 Here is Claude's plan:
 ╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌
 S74 Plan — Campaign Autopilot (strategy-agnostic rebalancer + prospect-adder)

 Context

 End of S73: 13 DRE sequences live in Outreach (2030–2042), all enabled, phase-a throttle applied (LQD-Universal 10/day, 12 others paused). Strategy-agnostic budget reporter ships at

 scripts/campaign_budget_status.py (read-only). The loop is not yet closed — no automation actually adds prospects or adjusts throttles on a schedule.

 S74 closes the loop. A daily job:

 1. Reads the reporter output for each configured strategy.

 2. Adjusts throttles against per-strategy weekly budgets (Tier 1 DRE slice = 266 sends/rolling-7-days, per memory/project_prioritization_plan_s66.md ).

 3. Pulls prospects from the 26,137-lead DRE pool and routes adds to the correct per-cohort sequence, enforcing Rule 17 (state + tz required).

 4. Sends a Telegram summary.

 Hard constraints (non-negotiable, from memory/feedback_automation_daily_not_hourly.md + memory/feedback_budget_automation_must_generalize.md ):

 - Daily at 7:00am OR 5:00pm CST, weekdays only — never hourly.

 - Strategy-agnostic — new module names campaign_* / tools/lead_filters.py / tools/grade_level_detector.py , NOT dre_* . Adding a new strategy = add a YAML entry, not a code change.

 - Respect Tier 1 DRE slice of 266/rolling-7d (not the full 4,250-email cap).

 - Rule 18 debt first : promote /tmp/sf_leads_dre_pass5.py + friends into tools/lead_filters.py + tools/grade_level_detector.py BEFORE any autopilot code depends on them.

 - Rule 17 : validate_prospect_inputs enforces state + IANA timezone ; missing either = skip contact (never fall back).

 - Rule 19 : no raw Outreach IDs in any Telegram text.

 - Rule 15 : autopilot never enables/activates sequences; it only PATCHes throttle Max Adds Per Day on already-enabled sequences.

 Design

 Architecture (all new files use campaign_* / strategy-agnostic naming)

 ┌──────────────── daily 07:00 or 17:00 CST ─────────────────┐

 │ │

 agent/main.py └─> scheduler.check() -> "campaign_autopilot" │

 │ │

 └─> _run_campaign_autopilot() │

 └─> scripts/campaign_autopilot.py --action both │

 │ │

 ├── tools/campaign_config.py (load YAML) │

 ├── scripts/campaign_budget_status.py (reuse) │

 ├── tools/campaign_rebalancer.py │

 │ └─ proposes throttle changes, │

 │ PATCHes when safe, flags when not │

 ├── tools/campaign_prospect_adder.py │

 │ ├─ tools/lead_filters.py (promoted) │

 │ ├─ tools/grade_level_detector.py (promoted)│

 │ ├─ tools/campaign_pool.py │

 │ └─ tools/prospect_loader.execute_load_plan │

 └── Telegram summary via tools.telegram_bot │

 Files to create / modify

 Promote Rule 18 debt (prerequisite — ship first commit):

 - tools/grade_level_detector.py (new) — extract detect_grade , map_grade_span , is_virtual , is_homeschool , homeschool_subsplit , is_code_ninjas , normalize_name from /tmp/sf_leads_dre_pass3.py + pass4.py +

 pass5.py . Pure functions, no I/O.

 - tools/lead_filters.py (new) — TC_SOURCES whitelist (22 strings from memory/project_uni_lead_source_whitelist.md ), build_territory_index_stateaware(sheets_svc) , fuzzy_match_v5(...) , classify_row_v5(...) ,

 classify_sf_leads_to_dre_buckets() -> dict[bucket -> list[lead]] . Lifts /tmp/sf_leads_dre_pass5.py verbatim into library form, with sheets reads gated behind a _load_sheets_service() helper so unit tests can

 mock it.

 - Unit tests under scripts/test_lead_filters.py lock classifier outputs against a small fixture set (5 known-bucket rows per bucket). Baseline: the 10 measured counts from PASS4/PASS5 at the top of pass5.py .

 New autopilot modules:

 - tools/campaign_config.py (new) — loads config/campaigns.yaml . Shape:

 strategies:

 dre:

 number: 12

 display: "DRE (Dormant Re-Engage)"

 tier: 1

 weekly_budget: 266

 sequence_ids_source: # pick one

 state_sidecar: data/dre_2026_spring.state.json

 pool:

 source: sf_leads

 classifier: lead_filters.classify_sf_leads_to_dre_buckets

 bucket_to_sequence:

 TC-Universal-Residual: 2031

 TC-MS: 2032

 TC-HS: 2033

 # ...

 throttle_profile: phase-a # named profile, name is authoritative

 profiles:

 phase-a: { LQD-Universal: 10 , default: 0 }

 proportional: { TC-Universal-Residual: 14 , TC-MS: 11 , ... }

 transition_rules:

 - when: "pool[LQD-Universal].remaining < 30"

 propose_profile: proportional # never auto-applied; surfaced in Telegram

 c4_cold_license:

 number: 10

 tier: 1

 weekly_budget: 266

 sequence_ids: [ 1995 , 1996 , 1997 , 1998 ]

 pool: null # empty for now — strategy has no pool wired yet

 - Replaces the inline STRATEGIES dict in scripts/campaign_budget_status.py (that file imports from campaign_config instead, preserving its CLI).

 - tools/campaign_pool.py (new) — thin wrapper around the classifier. Given strategy_key , returns {bucket_name: [{email, first_name, last_name, company, state, tz, sub_bucket}, ...]} . Persists to

 data/<strategy>_pool.jsonl (gitignored); refreshed on explicit --refresh-pool or when older than 7 days. Attaches IANA tz via tools.timezone_lookup.state_to_timezone at build time; drops rows with blank state

 OR no tz mapping (Rule 17 at the boundary, not per-add).

 - tools/campaign_rebalancer.py (new) —

 - compute_proposed_throttles(strategy_key, report, config) -> {sequence_id: new_throttle, ...}

 - apply_throttles(patches: dict, dry_run: bool) -> list[changelog_entry] — PATCH /sequences/<id> with {throttle Max Adds Per Day, throttle Capacity: 500, throttle Max Activations: 1} . Lifted from

 scripts/create_dre_sequences.py::configure_throttles (promote pattern per Rule 18).

 - Conservative policy for S74 first ship: only applies when the proposed throttle equals the named profile in config . Profile switches (phase-a → proportional) are proposed in Telegram with a one-liner

 Steven can paste back ( /autopilot apply-profile dre proportional ) — never auto-applied. This prevents runaway on the first strategy we automate.

 - tools/campaign_prospect_adder.py (new) — per enabled sequence with throttle Max Adds Per Day > 0 :

 a. need_today = throttle Max Adds Per Day - adds_already_made_today(seq_id) where the "already made" count comes from /sequence States?filter[sequence][id]=X&filter[created At]=TODAY_UTC.. .

 b. Pull need_today leads from campaign_pool[bucket] , dedupe by email across all sequences in the strategy.

 c. Build Load Plan list (existing tools/prospect_loader.Load Plan shape) with day_bucket=today_iso .

 d. Call tools.prospect_loader.execute_load_plan(plans, state_path=..., audit_path=..., sleep_seconds=(60, 120), verify_sequence_active=True, dry_run=...) .

 e. Hard caps: ≤ 100 adds per run across all strategies (safety fuse), ≤ 20 POSTs / 60s to any single sequence (already enforced by the sleep gap in execute_load_plan ).

 - scripts/campaign_autopilot.py (new CLI) — single entry point the scheduler dispatches to:

 campaign_autopilot.py --action {rebalance|add-prospects|both}

 --strategy {dre|all}

 [--dry-run]

 [--refresh-pool]

 [--apply-profile <name>] # Steven-only escape hatch

 - Default mode on scheduler wakeup = --action both --strategy all .

 - First week of live running defaults to --dry-run via env SCOUT_CAMPAIGN_AUTOPILOT_DRY_RUN=1 ; Steven flips it.

 - Kill switch: touch ~/.claude/state/scout-campaign-autopilot-disabled (symmetric with the rule-scanner kill switch).

 - Writes a structured JSONL audit log at vault/logs/campaign_autopilot.jsonl (one line per run).

 - agent/scheduler.py — add two ticks in the weekday block:

 if hour == 7 and minute == 0 :

 if self._last_campaign_autopilot_date != today:

 self._last_campaign_autopilot_date = today

 return "campaign_autopilot"

 - Keep 17:00 as a fallback trigger commented-out behind an env flag so Steven can swap with one line if 7am is the wrong hour. Plus _last_campaign_autopilot_date init in __init__ .

 - agent/main.py — register "campaign_autopilot" event handler _run_campaign_autopilot() modeled on _run_daily_signal_scan (lines 4042–4070 in main.py). Shells out to scripts/campaign_autopilot.py via

 run_in_executor (the script is synchronous), captures stdout summary, formats for Telegram, calls send_message() .

 - agent/CLAUDE.md — add "campaign_autopilot" to the scheduler event enum.

 Telegram output format (Rule 19-clean — no raw IDs):

 🤖 Campaign Autopilot — 2026-04-18 07:00 CST (dry run)

 DRE (Dormant Re-Engage) [Tier 1, 266/week budget]

 Throttles: held (profile: phase-a)

 Adds this run: 10 → LQD-Universal (397 remaining in cohort)

 C4 Cold License [Tier 1, 266/week budget]

 Note: no pool wired yet — skipped.

 Tier 1 TOTAL: 47 / 1,063 sends (4.4%), 1,016 headroom

 Build sequence (7 commits, per Rule 8)

 1. Promote /tmp/ Rule 18 debt → tools/grade_level_detector.py + tools/lead_filters.py + unit tests. No autopilot yet. Verifies bucket counts reproduce pass-5 baselines.

 2. tools/campaign_config.py + config/campaigns.yaml + refactor scripts/campaign_budget_status.py to consume it. Reporter output should be identical before/after. Add 2 tests.

 3. tools/campaign_pool.py + scripts/build_campaign_pool.py --strategy dre . Produces data/dre_pool.jsonl . Smoke test: row count matches S72 26,137 measured pool (±200 for tz-drop).

 4. tools/campaign_rebalancer.py + unit tests (mocked _api_patch ). Verify no writes in dry-run.

 5. tools/campaign_prospect_adder.py + unit tests (mocked execute_load_plan ). Verify Rule 17 skip paths, 100-total-adds fuse.

 6. scripts/campaign_autopilot.py CLI tying 2–5 together. Run once with --dry-run locally against live Outreach reads. Verify Telegram formatter output.

 7. Scheduler + agent/main.py wiring + env flag default SCOUT_CAMPAIGN_AUTOPILOT_DRY_RUN=1 . Ship. Watch for 1 daily tick. Steven flips the env to go live.

 Files to modify (existing)

 - scripts/campaign_budget_status.py — delegate STRATEGIES to tools.campaign_config . CLI preserved.

 - agent/scheduler.py — add 1 weekday tick (7:00am CST). 5 lines.

 - agent/main.py — add _run_campaign_autopilot() handler + dispatch. ~40 lines.

 - agent/CLAUDE.md — add event name to docs.

 - .gitignore — add data/*_pool.jsonl and vault/logs/campaign_autopilot.jsonl .

 Files to reuse (no modification)

 - tools/outreach_client.py::validate_prospect_inputs / find_prospect_by_email / add_prospect_to_sequence / _api_patch / _api_get / _api_get_all

 - tools/prospect_loader.py::execute_load_plan / LoadPlan

 - tools/timezone_lookup.py::state_to_timezone

 - tools/telegram_bot.py::send_message

 - scripts/create_dre_sequences.py::PHASE_A_ADDS_PER_DAY / PROPORTIONAL_ADDS_PER_DAY (still authoritative source, YAML references profile names)

 Verification

 1. Unit tests (commit 1) : .venv/bin/python -m pytest scripts/test_lead_filters.py — fixture rows map to the 10 buckets in PASS4 dict at pass5.py:44.

 2. Budget reporter parity (commit 2) : diff <(git checkout HEAD~1 -- scripts/campaign_budget_status.py; .venv/bin/python scripts/campaign_budget_status.py --format json) <(.venv/bin/python

 scripts/campaign_budget_status.py --format json) — identical JSON.

 3. Pool builder smoke (commit 3) : .venv/bin/python scripts/build_campaign_pool.py --strategy dre --dry-run — logs measured row count against S72's 26,137 baseline. Delta ≤ 200 acceptable (tz drops).

 4. Rebalancer dry-run (commit 4) : .venv/bin/python scripts/campaign_autopilot.py --action rebalance --strategy dre --dry-run — produces proposed-patch changelog; no _api_patch calls (verified by test mock).

 5. Adder dry-run (commit 5) : .venv/bin/python scripts/campaign_autopilot.py --action add-prospects --strategy dre --dry-run — prints LoadPlan summary for today: 10 LQD-Universal adds, 0 others.

 6. End-to-end local (commit 6) : SCOUT_CAMPAIGN_AUTOPILOT_DRY_RUN=1 .venv/bin/python scripts/campaign_autopilot.py --action both — produces Telegram-formatted summary, confirms no writes (Rule 19: no raw IDs in

 output — grep returned text for sequence_id|prospect_id|\b\d{4}\b and visually confirm names only).

 7. Live scheduler tick (commit 7) : Wait for 7:00am CST next weekday. Expect Telegram message. First run is dry; Steven flips SCOUT_CAMPAIGN_AUTOPILOT_DRY_RUN=0 after inspecting. Second live run should show

 real adds this run: N → <sequence name> and Outreach UI should reflect new prospects in LQD-Universal sequence.

 Non-goals (explicitly deferred)

 - Auto-switching throttle profiles ( phase-a → proportional ). First version proposes only ; Steven runs --apply-profile manually.

 - Wiring strategies #9 / #11 (empty sequence lists). Config has placeholders; no pool logic.

 - Generalizing the classifier to non-DRE strategies. S74 ships the framework ; DRE is the first client. Other strategies fill their own pool.classifier config entry in future sessions.

 - Moving PHASE_A_ADDS_PER_DAY / PROPORTIONAL_ADDS_PER_DAY out of create_dre_sequences.py into YAML. The YAML references them by name for now; full extraction waits until a 2nd strategy needs its own profiles.

 - Real-time reply-rate based throttle tuning. Deferred to after 2–3 weeks of live send data per memory/project_prioritization_plan_s66.md .

 ❯ T ell Claude what to change

 Refine this plan. Your job is to pressure-test it honestly — keep what's
 genuinely strong, rebuild what isn't, and hand back something I can execute
 with full confidence.
 Act as a ruthless, senior-level technical reviewer and architect. Be direct,

 specific, and opinionated. No hedging, no diplomacy, no soft language around

 real problems. If something is wrong, say it's wrong and say why. But the

 inverse also holds: if something is right, leave it alone. Do not rewrite solid

 work just to look thorough — churn for its own sake is its own kind of

 failure. The bar for changing something is that the new version is meaningfully

 better, not merely different.

 happen because the reviewer started reacting before they finished

 understanding.

 1. Challenge the foundation. Is the overall approach actually right, or is it

 just the first thing that came to mind? Interrogate the architecture, the

 framing, and the core assumptions before touching details. If there's a

 fundamentally better angle — different architecture, different sequencing,

 different tradeoffs, a smaller problem hiding inside the big one — say so and

 restructure around it. Do not anchor to your first answer because you already

 committed to it on the page.

 2. Stress-test every layer as if you're executing it tomorrow. Walk the plan

 end to end and ask: could I actually sit down and build this without hitting a

 wall? Find the hidden assumptions, the vague steps, the dependency tangles, the

 places where things will quietly break at the edges, the spots where "it'll

 just work" is doing real load-bearing work. For every problem: name it, explain

 why it happens, explain what it costs, and give the fix. Diagnosis without

 prescription is useless.

 3. Pressure-test every technical decision against the best available knowledge.

 Architecture, tooling, patterns, data modeling, sequencing — for each one, ask

 whether it's genuinely the strongest option or just the most familiar. Weigh

 it against what actually holds up in production, not what's popular in

 tutorials or trending this quarter. Where real tradeoffs exist between

 competing approaches, surface them honestly and take a clear position on which

 one I should pick and why. No "it depends" cop-outs.

 4. Optimize for the highest-quality result, reached as fast and efficiently as

 possible. Every step should be pulling its weight toward a correct, durable,

 high-quality outcome — not just toward motion. Ruthlessly cut anything that

 adds time without adding quality, and ruthlessly add anything that protects

 quality even if it costs a little time upfront. Sequence the work so that the

 decisions and pieces with the highest leverage on the final result come first,

 so that mistakes get caught as early and cheaply as possible, and so that

 nothing downstream has to be redone because something upstream was rushed.

 Where two paths exist, pick the one that gets to the correct answer with the

 least wasted motion — and say why. Speed comes from not doing the wrong work,

 not from skipping the right work.

 5. Cut the fat, keep the muscle. Depth is welcome. Redundancy, vagueness, and

 ceremony are not. Every element earns its place or it goes. If a step would

 make me stop and ask "what does this actually mean in practice," either make it

 concrete or delete it. If two steps do the same work, collapse them. If a

 section exists because it sounds thorough rather than because it changes the

 outcome, kill it.

 6. Fill the gaps a senior engineer would catch in a design review. What's

 missing that looks minor now and will hurt later? Edge cases, error handling,

 failure modes, prerequisites, migration paths, ordering constraints, and the

 easy-to-punt decisions that get expensive to reverse. Surface them now, while

 they're cheap.

 7. Rewrite the full plan from scratch. Do not patch the original — rebuild it

 with everything above baked in. Make it comprehensive, precise, and immediately

 actionable. Preserve the parts of the original that were already strong —

 rebuilding does not mean discarding what worked. The goal is the best possible

 plan, not the most rewritten one. Then, at the end, list the most important

 changes you made and explain the reasoning behind each one so I can judge

 whether your thinking holds up.

 Take your time. Think deeply. This is the version I'm going to execute from —

 make it the best one you're capable of producing.

 Ready to code?

 Here is Claude's plan:
 ╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌
 S74 — Campaign Autopilot (Rewritten)

 Context

 End of S73: 13 DRE sequences live ( 2030–2042 ), throttle profile phase-a applied (LQD-Universal 10/day, 12 others paused). scripts/campaign_budget_status.py reports rolling-7-day send volume per strategy. No

 automation adds prospects. No automation adjusts throttles.

 The goal of S74 is narrow and load-bearing: a daily job that adds prospects from the 26,137-lead DRE pool into the correct cohort sequence, on a strategy-agnostic foundation that extends to the remaining 23

 strategies . Everything beyond that is out of scope.

 Non-negotiable constraints (from Steven's explicit S73 feedback):

 - Daily 07:00 CST weekdays. Not hourly. Not 17:00 as well. One tick.

 - Strategy-agnostic modules — names campaign_* , tools/lead_filters.py , tools/grade_level_detector.py . Adding a new strategy is config-only.

 - Respect Tier 1 DRE slice: 266 sends / rolling-7-days (NOT the 4,250 weekly user cap).

 - Rule 17: state + IANA timezone required per prospect. Missing either → skip, never fall back.

 - Rule 18: promote /tmp/sf_leads_dre_pass*.py classifiers into tools/ BEFORE any dependent code ships.

 - Rule 19: no raw Outreach numeric IDs in any Telegram output.

 - Rule 15: autopilot never enables sequences. Only PATCHes throttle on already-enabled sequences.

 - Telegram notifications on throttle changes + adds.

 Foundation decisions (what changed vs first draft, and why)

 1. Kill the auto-rebalancer module for S74. Ship a headroom report only.

 Phase-a is deliberately static: LQD drains over ~8 weeks, then Steven switches to proportional . There is nothing for a rebalancer to do during S74's first 6 weeks. Building a speculative rebalancer module +

 config layer + tests for a system that only acts once-per-quarter is negative-value work — it will rot before it runs. The daily summary includes proposed profile switches as text (e.g. "LQD cohort at 43

 remaining — consider /apply_profile dre proportional in ~4 weeks"). Actual throttle PATCHes reuse the existing create_dre_sequences.py::configure_throttles function, invoked manually by Steven when the trigger

 fires. When a second strategy develops real transition needs, then extract tools/campaign_rebalancer.py .

 2. Plain Python config, not YAML.

 3 real strategies today. The inline STRATEGIES dict at scripts/campaign_budget_status.py:78-109 is 30 lines of clear Python. YAML adds a loader, a schema, a dependency, and indirection for zero net clarity at

 this scale. Extract the dict to tools/campaign_config.py as a plain Python module; both the reporter and the autopilot import from it. Python-as-config is the right pattern until we hit ~15 strategies with

 heterogeneous profile shapes.

 3. No subprocess shell-out.

 tools/campaign_autopilot.py exposes run_autopilot(strategy_key="all", dry_run, refresh_pool) -> AutopilotReport . The CLI is a thin __main__ block. agent/main.py::_run_campaign_autopilot imports and calls the

 function directly. Subprocess launches and stdout parsing would duplicate the existing _run_daily_signal_scan shape without benefit.

 4. Safety fuse is sum(active throttles) , not a magic 100.

 If phase-a says LQD=10 and 12 others=0, today's fuse is 10. No overshoot possible by design. A literal "100/day" cap is either redundant (when active throttles sum ≤100) or dangerous (when they sum >100 and

 100 becomes an arbitrary under-cap).

 5. Pool refresh: Monday 07:00 CST only.

 Predictable cadence beats age-based heuristics. Tue–Fri uses the Monday-built pool. Pool rebuild cost is ~2 min (26k row classify); no reason to amortize.

 6. Rule 17 tagging, not silent drop.

 Pool builder classifies every row AND tags disqualified rows with reason ( disqualified_no_state , disqualified_no_tz_for_state , disqualified_90d_recent_touch ). Summary surfaces counts. Silent drops are

 debugging hell the first time Steven asks "why only 10 adds from a 26k pool?"

 7. "Adds already today" uses 24h-back-from-now filter.

 Outreach's throttle Max Adds Per Day day boundary is not documented. Querying /sequence States?filter[created At]=24h_ago.. is strictly conservative and UTC-math-free — if yesterday 5pm had an add, it counts against

 today. Minor under-send on days after manual bursts. Acceptable.

 8. First production run is human-gated.

 Autopilot ships with SCOUT_CAMPAIGN_AUTOPILOT_MODE=dry_run in env. Scheduler still fires daily and posts a Telegram summary showing what would have happened . Going live requires Steven to type /autopilot_live

 in Telegram — a new slash command that flips an in-memory flag + sets the env default. This separates "does the code work" from "is Scout actually sending." Restarting the bot resets the flag (fail-safe

 default).

 9. Telegram formatter is the Rule 19 boundary.

 Formatter takes dicts keyed by sequence_name , never takes raw IDs as text input. Makes "accidentally leak an ID" structurally impossible — no regex sanitization needed. The IDs flow through data structures,

 not through format strings.

 10. Sleep cadence 60-120s between POSTs, not 300-900s.

 prospect_loader.execute_load_plan default was tuned for diocesan human-paced drip. Autopilot with 10 adds/day needs <20 min wall time. 60-120s keeps Outreach happy (<20 POSTs/minute per the preflight rule),

 finishes before morning brief at 09:15.

 Architecture

 agent/main.py poll loop

 └─> scheduler.check() returns "campaign_autopilot" at 07:00 CST weekday

 └─> _run_campaign_autopilot()

 └─> tools.campaign_autopilot.run_autopilot(dry_run=<env>)

 ├─> tools.campaign_config.STRATEGIES (plain dict)

 ├─> scripts.campaign_budget_status.build_strategy_report() (reuse)

 ├─> tools.campaign_pool.get_or_build_pool(strategy_key, refresh=<Mon?>)

 │ └─> tools.lead_filters.classify_sf_leads_to_dre_buckets()

 │ └─> tools.grade_level_detector.* (pure functions)

 ├─> per-sequence: compute need_today → build LoadPlan list

 ├─> tools.prospect_loader.execute_load_plan(plans, dry_run)

 └─> format_telegram_summary() → tools.telegram_bot.send_message()

 Files

 New modules (promoted from /tmp/ or net-new)

 tools/grade_level_detector.py — pure functions lifted from /tmp/sf_leads_dre_pass3.py and pass4.py :

 - detect_grade(company: str, verified_school: str) -> Literal["Elem","MS","HS","All-Grades","Unknown"]

 - map_grade_span(span: str) -> Optional[Literal["Elem","MS","HS","AllGrades"]]

 - is_virtual(company, verified) -> bool

 - is_homeschool(company) -> bool

 - homeschool_subsplit(company) -> str (returns bucket name)

 - is_code_ninjas(company) -> bool

 - normalize_name(name) -> str

 Zero I/O. Unit tests with string inputs.

 tools/lead_filters.py — fuzzy-match + classifier logic, I/O gated behind a sheets-service-factory:

 - TC_SOURCES: frozenset[str] (the 22 whitelisted Lead Source strings from memory/project_uni_lead_source_whitelist.md )

 - build_territory_index_stateaware(svc) -> Territory Index (Named Tuple holding the 7 indexes from pass5.py)

 - fuzzy_match_v5(company, state, idx, counters) -> tuple[Optional[bucket], strategy_tag]

 - classify_row(company, verified, state, title, source, idx) -> Optional[bucket_name] (returns None if row is excluded — blank title, wrong source, code ninjas)

 - classify_sf_leads_to_dre_buckets(sheets_svc=None) -> Dre Pool Result where Dre Pool Result has .buckets: dict[str, list[Lead Row]] , .disqualified: dict[reason, int] , .counters: dict .

 tools/campaign_config.py — plain Python:

 STRATEGIES: dict [ str , StrategyConfig] = { ... }

 # Migrated from scripts/campaign_budget_status.py, plus autopilot-relevant keys:

 # pool_source: "sf_leads_dre" | None

 # bucket_to_sequence_name: {"LQD-Universal": "LQD-Universal", ...} or None

 # throttle_profile_name: "phase-a" | "proportional" | None

 class Strategy Config(Typed Dict, total= False ):

 number: int

 display: str

 tier: int

 weekly_budget: int

 sequence_ids: list [ int ]

 sequence_ids_loader: Callable [[], list [ int ]]

 pool_source: Optional [ str ] # None = no pool wired

 bucket_to_sequence_name: Optional [ dict [ str , str ]]

 throttle_profile_name: Optional [ str ] # references create_dre_sequences profile names

 tools/campaign_pool.py — the I/O boundary:

 - get_or_build_pool(strategy_key, refresh=False) -> StrategyPool

 - build_pool(strategy_key) -> StrategyPool — dispatches on pool_source . For "sf_leads_dre" : calls lead_filters.classify_sf_leads_to_dre_buckets , enriches each row with state_to_timezone , filters out

 90-day-recent-touch prospects via bulk Outreach /mailings query on company,email , tags disqualified rows, writes JSONL + metadata sidecar at data/<strategy_key>_pool.jsonl .

 - Strategy Pool dataclass holds buckets: dict[bucket_name, list[Pool Lead]] , disqualified: dict[reason, int] , built_at: str , total_eligible: int .

 - PoolLead dataclass mirrors prospect_loader.Contact for zero-friction conversion.

 tools/campaign_autopilot.py — the orchestrator:

 - run_autopilot(strategy_key="all", dry_run=True, refresh_pool=None) -> AutopilotReport

 - refresh_pool=None → auto (Monday: rebuild, else reuse)

 - For each configured strategy with pool_source : builds per-sequence LoadPlan lists, calls execute_load_plan , collects results.

 - Kill switch checked FIRST: Path("~/.claude/state/scout-campaign-autopilot-disabled").expanduser().exists() → returns early with a "disabled" report.

 - compute_need_per_sequence(strategy, report) -> dict[seq_id, int]

 - need = throttle Max Adds Per Day - adds_in_last_24h

 - adds_in_last_24h = _api_get_all("/sequence States", filter[sequence][id]=seq_id & filter[created At]=now-24h..)

 - _compute_safety_fuse(need_dict) -> int — returns sum(need_dict.values()) . No magic caps.

 - format_telegram_summary(report, dry_run) -> str — takes AutopilotReport (dict-keyed by sequence name , not ID). Formatter has no parameter of type int that could be a sequence ID — enforced structurally.

 - CLI __main__ : --strategy , --dry-run / --live , --refresh-pool , --pool-only (build and exit).

 scripts/build_campaign_pool.py — thin wrapper that calls campaign_pool.build_pool(strategy_key) and prints summary. Used for manual refreshes outside the Monday auto-rebuild.

 agent/scheduler.py — add to __init__ : self._last_campaign_autopilot_date = None . Add to check() inside weekday block:

 if hour == 7 and minute == 0 :

 if self._last_campaign_autopilot_date != today:

 self._last_campaign_autopilot_date = today

 return "campaign_autopilot"

 agent/main.py — add _run_campaign_autopilot() modeled on _run_daily_signal_scan (main.py:4042). Dispatch mapping inside the scheduler-check branch (grep for "signal_scan" dispatch pattern). Add /autopilot_live

 slash command handler that flips _autopilot_live_flag in-memory (resets on bot restart, fail-safe to dry-run).

 agent/CLAUDE.md — add "campaign_autopilot" to scheduler event enum.

 scripts/campaign_budget_status.py — replace inline STRATEGIES with from tools.campaign_config import STRATEGIES . CLI behavior unchanged.

 .gitignore — add data/*_pool.jsonl , data/*_pool.meta.json , vault/logs/campaign_autopilot.jsonl .

 Build sequence (6 commits, Rule 8)

 Each commit is independently shippable and reversible via git revert .

 Commit 1 — tools/grade_level_detector.py + tests.

 - Lift pure functions from /tmp/sf_leads_dre_pass3.py + pass4.py .

 - scripts/test_grade_level_detector.py — fixture: 30 company strings from the S72 classification sample, assert bucket outputs match the PASS4 baseline in pass5.py:44.

 - Verify: .venv/bin/python -m pytest scripts/test_grade_level_detector.py -v — all pass.

 Commit 2 — tools/lead_filters.py + tests + pool-size parity check.

 - Move fuzzy-match + classifier from /tmp/sf_leads_dre_pass5.py .

 - scripts/test_lead_filters.py — mocks the Sheets service, feeds a 50-row fixture, asserts bucket counts match hand-tallied truth.

 - Live integration: python -c "from tools.lead_filters import classify_sf_leads_to_dre_buckets; r = classify_sf_leads_to_dre_buckets(); print({k: len(v) for k,v in r.buckets.items()})" — must reproduce pass5

 bucket distribution within ±50 rows per bucket (measured baselines in pass5.py:44).

 Commit 3 — tools/campaign_config.py + campaign_budget_status.py refactor.

 - Extract STRATEGIES dict to tools.campaign_config . Reporter imports from it.

 - Add autopilot-relevant keys to DRE + C4 entries: pool_source , bucket_to_sequence_name (DRE only — maps all 13 buckets), throttle_profile_name .

 - Verify: diff <(git stash; python scripts/campaign_budget_status.py --format json; git stash pop) <(python scripts/campaign_budget_status.py --format json) — byte-identical.

 Commit 4 — tools/campaign_pool.py + scripts/build_campaign_pool.py .

 - Wires lead_filters classifier + timezone_lookup + /mailings 90-day touch filter.

 - Writes data/dre_2026_spring_pool.jsonl + .meta.json sidecar.

 - Verify:

 - python scripts/build_campaign_pool.py --strategy dre produces JSONL.

 - jq 'length' data/dre_2026_spring_pool.jsonl within ±300 of 26,137 (tz + recent-touch drops).

 - Every row has non-empty state AND tz .

 - Metadata sidecar shows disqualified-reason breakdown totaling (raw_classified − kept).

 Commit 5 — tools/campaign_autopilot.py + dry-run end-to-end.

 - Implements run_autopilot , compute_need_per_sequence , format_telegram_summary , kill-switch check.

 - CLI python -m tools.campaign_autopilot --dry-run --strategy dre :

 - Loads pool, computes 10-need for LQD-Universal (throttle=10, 0 adds in last 24h).

 - Builds 10 LoadPlans.

 - Calls execute_load_plan(..., dry_run=True) — no POSTs.

 - Prints Telegram summary to stdout. Visually confirm: no integer in range [2017, 2050] appears. Grep: python -m tools.campaign_autopilot --dry-run 2>&1 | grep -oE '\b20[1-4][0-9]\b' → empty.

 Commit 6 — agent/scheduler.py + agent/main.py wire-up + /autopilot_live command.

 - Scheduler returns "campaign_autopilot" at 07:00 CST weekday.

 - _run_campaign_autopilot() imports tools.campaign_autopilot , calls run_autopilot(dry_run=not _autopilot_live_flag) , formats result, sends to Telegram.

 - /autopilot_live slash command flips _autopilot_live_flag = True . Replies with confirmation "Autopilot live mode ON — next 07:00 tick will POST real adds. Restart Scout to reset to dry-run."

 - /autopilot_dry slash command flips it back.

 - Verify:

 - Restart Scout locally. Wait for the 07:00 CST tick (or manually invoke _run_campaign_autopilot() via a one-off REPL) — Telegram gets a dry-run summary.

 - Send /autopilot_live in Telegram — confirmation reply. Flag flipped.

 - Manually re-invoke — live summary shows real adds from the 10-prospect LQD-Universal batch.

 - Confirm in Outreach UI: 10 new prospects in LQD-Universal sequence.

 Audit log shape

 vault/logs/campaign_autopilot.jsonl — one line per run:

 {

 "ts_utc" : "2026-04-18T12:00:00Z" ,

 "ts_cst" : "2026-04-18T07:00:00-05:00" ,

 "mode" : "dry_run|live|disabled" ,

 "strategies" : {

 "dre" : {

 "budget_7d" : 266 , "sends_7d" : 47 , "headroom" : 219 ,

 "sequences" : {

 "LQD-Universal" : { "throttle" : 10 , "adds_last_24h" : 0 , "need" : 10 ,

 "attempted" : 10 , "succeeded" : 10 , "skipped_rule17" : 0 ,

 "skipped_dedup" : 0 , "failed" : 0 }

 },

 "pool_age_hours" : 2.3 ,

 "pool_disqualified" : { "no_state" : 312 , "no_tz" : 4 , "recent_touch" : 1876 }

 }

 },

 "errors" : []

 }

 Telegram format

 🤖 Campaign Autopilot — 2026-04-18 07:00 CST

 [DRY RUN — send /autopilot_live to enable real sends]

 DRE (Dormant Re-Engage) Tier 1

 Sends 7d: 47/266 (17.7%), 219 headroom

 Profile: phase-a (held)

 Would add 10 → LQD-Universal (cohort: 397 remaining)

 All 12 other DRE sequences paused — no adds.

 C4 Cold License Tier 1

 Sends 7d: 0/266 (0%), 266 headroom

 Tier 1 TOTAL: 47/1,063 sends, 1,016 headroom

 Live mode replaces [DRY RUN …] with [LIVE] and "Would add" → "Added".

 Verification (end-to-end golden path)

 On commit 6 ship day, with bot freshly restarted:

 1. ls ~/.claude/state/scout-campaign-autopilot-disabled → does not exist.

 2. Wait for 07:00 CST weekday tick (or manually invoke in a Python REPL for iteration).

 3. Telegram receives [DRY RUN] summary; vault/logs/campaign_autopilot.jsonl has a new line with mode="dry_run" .

 4. Grep last-line: tail -1 vault/logs/campaign_autopilot.jsonl | python -c "import sys,json; d=json.loads(sys.stdin.read()); assert d['strategies']['dre']['sequences']['LQD-Universal']['attempted']==10 and

 d['strategies']['dre']['sequences']['LQD-Universal']['succeeded']==0" — succeeded=0 in dry-run.

 5. Send /autopilot_live in Telegram. Receive confirmation.

 6. Manually re-invoke _run_campaign_autopilot() via python -c "import asyncio; from agent.main import _run_campaign_autopilot; asyncio.run(_run_campaign_autopilot())" .

 7. Telegram receives [LIVE] summary with "Added 10 → LQD-Universal".

 8. Confirm in Outreach UI (read-only): LQD-Universal sequence shows 10 new active prospects, all with populated State + Timezone on the prospect record.

 9. tail -1 vault/logs/campaign_autopilot.jsonl shows mode="live" and succeeded=10 .

 10. Restart Scout → _autopilot_live_flag resets to False → next tick defaults to dry-run.

 Non-goals (explicitly deferred)

 - tools/campaign_rebalancer.py auto-PATCH module. Defer to the session where LQD cohort drains (measured: ~8 weeks out).

 - YAML config format. Defer until ~15 strategies with heterogeneous profile shapes.

 - Profile-switch slash command ( /apply_profile ). Defer to S75+; interim path is manual scripts/create_dre_sequences.py --configure-throttles proportional .

 - Wiring pools for strategies #9 / #11. Those strategies have empty sequence_ids ; config has pool_source=None so autopilot skips them cleanly.

 - Reply-rate-driven throttle tuning. Defer until 2–3 weeks of live send data exist.

 - Generalizing the classifier to non-DRE strategies. Each future strategy's pool_source string maps to its own classifier function. S74 ships sf_leads_dre only.

 - Subsuming create_dre_sequences.py::configure_throttles into tools/ . It's already library-shaped enough; the existing CLI works. Promote when the second strategy wants its own profiles.

 Risks & mitigations

 ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────┬──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐

 │ Risk │ Mitigation │

 ├─────────────────────────────────────────────────┼──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤

 │ Autopilot POSTs adds to wrong sequence │ bucket_to_sequence_name is config-validated at module load (every bucket maps to a real sequence name; name resolves to an enabled=True ID via │

 │ │ campaign_budget_status.get_sequence_meta ) │

 ├─────────────────────────────────────────────────┼──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤

 │ Outreach throttle Max Adds Per Day day boundary │ Strictly conservative: under-sends slightly on days after manual bursts. Acceptable for first shipment; revisit if Outreach returns 429s (observed in │

 │ differs from our 24h-back query │ audit log) │

 ├─────────────────────────────────────────────────┼──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤

 │ Pool builder diverges from S72's 26,137 count │ Commit 2 live integration requires reproducing pass-5 bucket totals within ±50/bucket before shipping. Commit 4 asserts total within ±300 │

 │ │ post-Rule-17-drops │

 ├─────────────────────────────────────────────────┼──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤

 │ Bot restart during autopilot execution drops │ execute_load_plan writes state file after every POST, reruns are idempotent by email. Autopilot re-entry on next day's tick skips already-done plans via │

 │ mid-run state │ day_bucket mismatch (yesterday's plans don't match today's day_bucket) — acceptable loss: at most ~10 adds deferred by a day │

 ├─────────────────────────────────────────────────┼──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤

 │ Rule 19 slip in Telegram output │ Formatter parameter types exclude raw IDs entirely. Test: commit 5 grep grep -oE '\b20[1-4][0-9]\b' returns empty on dry-run stdout │

 ├─────────────────────────────────────────────────┼──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤

 │ Dry-run env flag accidentally flipped to live │ Two gates: env default is dry_run , AND _autopilot_live_flag starts False on every bot restart. Both must be opt-in to send │

 ├─────────────────────────────────────────────────┼──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤

 │ Sheets API rate limit during Monday pool │ pass5 reads SF Leads + Territory Schools in 1 batchGet each (~2 calls). Well under quota. Fallback: Monday rebuild retry with exponential backoff, fall │

 │ rebuild │ through to Friday's pool if retries fail │

 ├─────────────────────────────────────────────────┼──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤

 │ execute_load_plan 1-2 min sleeps cause run to │ With max need = 10 adds × 120s = 20 min, run ends by 07:20 well before 08:15. Cap total run at max(need × 120, 45 * 60) seconds — if we ever blow past │

 │ exceed the 08:15 RFP-scan tick │ 45 min, log & bail │

 └─────────────────────────────────────────────────┴──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

 Change summary (from first draft)

 ┌─────┬──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┬─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐

 │ # │ Change │ Why │

 ├─────┼──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┼─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤

 │ 1 │ Cut the auto-rebalancer module │ Nothing for it to do during S74's 6-week phase-a window. Speculative infrastructure rots │

 ├─────┼──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┼─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤

 │ 2 │ Python module for config, not YAML │ 3 strategies. YAML overhead > clarity payoff at this scale │

 ├─────┼──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┼─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤

 │ 3 │ Direct function call, not subprocess shell-out │ Matches existing _run_daily_signal_scan pattern. Simpler tests, no stdout-parsing fragility │

 ├─────┼──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┼─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤

 │ 4 │ Safety fuse = sum(active throttles) │ 100/day was arbitrary and sometimes wrong-direction │

 ├─────┼──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┼─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤

 │ 5 │ Pool refresh only on Monday 07:00 │ Predictable cadence beats age heuristics │

 ├─────┼──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┼─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤

 │ 6 │ Tag disqualified rows, don't drop │ Debugging hell when Steven asks "why only N from 26k?" │

 ├─────┼──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┼─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤

 │ 7 │ 24h-back filter, not CST-midnight math │ Simpler, strictly conservative, zero tz bug surface │

 ├─────┼──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┼─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤

 │ 8 │ /autopilot_live human gate + fail-safe flag reset on restart │ Separates "code works" from "Scout sends." First live run is an explicit act │

 ├─────┼──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┼─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤

 │ 9 │ Formatter excludes raw IDs by type │ Structural Rule 19 enforcement, not regex-scrubbing │

 ├─────┼──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┼─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤

 │ 10 │ 60-120s sleeps, not 300-900s │ Finishes before morning brief. Diocesan human-pace was wrong model │

 ├─────┼──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┼─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤

 │ 11 │ 6 commits, not 7 (rebalancer cut) │ Less code, same value │

 ├─────┼──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┼─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤

 │ 12 │ Each commit has a specific golden-path verification │ Catches regressions at the commit boundary, not at end-of-session │

 ├─────┼──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┼─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤

 │ 13 │ Added risks table with concrete mitigations │ Senior-review hygiene the first draft was missing │

 └─────┴──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┴─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

 [<u[>1u[>4;2m

 Refine this plan. Your job is to pressure-test it
 honestly — keep what's genuinely strong, rebuild what isn't, and hand back
 something I can execute with full confidence.
 Act as a ruthless, senior-level technical reviewer and architect. Be direct,

 specific, and opinionated. No hedging, no diplomacy, no soft language around

 real problems. If something is wrong, say it's wrong and say why. But the

 inverse also holds: if something is right, leave it alone. Do not rewrite solid

 work just to look thorough — churn for its own sake is its own kind of

 failure. The bar for changing something is that the new version is meaningfully

 better, not merely different.

 happen because the reviewer started reacting before they finished

 understanding.

 1. Challenge the foundation. Is the overall approach actually right, or is it

 just the first thing that came to mind? Interrogate the architecture, the

 framing, and the core assumptions before touching details. If there's a

 fundamentally better angle — different architecture, different sequencing,

 different tradeoffs, a smaller problem hiding inside the big one — say so and

 restructure around it. Do not anchor to your first answer because you already

 committed to it on the page.

 2. Stress-test every layer as if you're executing it tomorrow. Walk the plan

 end to end and ask: could I actually sit down and build this without hitting a

 wall? Find the hidden assumptions, the vague steps, the dependency tangles, the

 places where things will quietly break at the edges, the spots where "it'll

 just work" is doing real load-bearing work. For every problem: name it, explain

 why it happens, explain what it costs, and give the fix. Diagnosis without

 prescription is useless.

 3. Pressure-test every technical decision against the best available knowledge.

 Architecture, tooling, patterns, data modeling, sequencing — for each one, ask

 whether it's genuinely the strongest option or just the most familiar. Weigh

 it against what actually holds up in production, not what's popular in

 tutorials or trending this quarter. Where real tradeoffs exist between

 competing approaches, surface them honestly and take a clear position on which

 one I should pick and why. No "it depends" cop-outs.

 4. Optimize for the highest-quality result, reached as fast and efficiently as

 possible. Every step should be pulling its weight toward a correct, durable,

 high-quality outcome — not just toward motion. Ruthlessly cut anything that

 adds time without adding quality, and ruthlessly add anything that protects

 quality even if it costs a little time upfront. Sequence the work so that the

 decisions and pieces with the highest leverage on the final result come first,

 so that mistakes get caught as early and cheaply as possible, and so that

 nothing downstream has to be redone because something upstream was rushed.

 Where two paths exist, pick the one that gets to the correct answer with the

 least wasted motion — and say why. Speed comes from not doing the wrong work,

 not from skipping the right work.

 5. Cut the fat, keep the muscle. Depth is welcome. Redundancy, vagueness, and

 ceremony are not. Every element earns its place or it goes. If a step would

 make me stop and ask "what does this actually mean in practice," either make it

 concrete or delete it. If two steps do the same work, collapse them. If a

 section exists because it sounds thorough rather than because it changes the

 outcome, kill it.

 6. Fill the gaps a senior engineer would catch in a design review. What's

 missing that looks minor now and will hurt later? Edge cases, error handling,

 failure modes, prerequisites, migration paths, ordering constraints, and the

 easy-to-punt decisions that get expensive to reverse. Surface them now, while

 they're cheap.

 7. Rewrite the full plan from scratch. Do not patch the original — rebuild it

 with everything above baked in. Make it comprehensive, precise, and immediately

 actionable. Preserve the parts of the original that were already strong —

 rebuilding does not mean discarding what worked. The goal is the best possible

 plan, not the most rewritten one. Then, at the end, list the most important

 changes you made and explain the reasoning behind each one so I can judge

 whether your thinking holds up.

 Take your time. Think deeply. This is the version I'm going to execute from —

 make it the best one you're capable of producing.

 emails/rolling-7d, (4) pull from 26,137-lead pool (promote /tmp/ S72 filter artifacts to tools/lead_filters.py + tools/grade_level_detector.py first per Rule 18), (5) Rule 17 state+tz enforced per prospect,

 Ready to code?

 Here is Claude's plan:
 ╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌
 S74 — Campaign Autopilot

 Context

 S73 left us with 13 DRE sequences live ( 2030–2042 ), throttle profile phase-a applied (LQD-Universal 10/day, 12 others 0), and a read-only budget reporter at scripts/campaign_budget_status.py . No automation

 adds prospects. The 26,137-lead DRE pool only exists as one-shot scripts in /tmp/ .

 S74 ships one worker: a daily 07:00 CST job that pulls from the DRE pool and adds prospects to the correct cohort sequence, on a strategy-agnostic foundation. That's it. No auto-rebalancer, no YAML, no

 profile-switch UX — those are speculative until LQD drains in ~8 weeks.

 Non-negotiables (Steven's S73 feedback):

 - Daily 07:00 CST weekdays. One tick.

 - Strategy-agnostic ( campaign_* , lead_filters , grade_level_detector ). New strategy = config-only.

 - Respect Tier 1 DRE slice: 266 sends / rolling-7-days.

 - Rule 17: state + IANA tz required → missing = skip.

 - Rule 18: promote /tmp/sf_leads_dre_pass*.py into tools/ FIRST.

 - Rule 19: no raw IDs in Telegram output.

 - Rule 15: never enable sequences; only PATCH throttle on enabled ones.

 - Telegram summary on every tick.

 Foundation decisions

 1. Scope: prospect-adder only. No rebalancer in S74.

 Phase-a is static for ~8 weeks. An auto-rebalancer has no trigger during that window. Build it when a trigger fires, not before. Profile switches remain manual via the existing scripts/create_dre_sequences.py

 --configure-throttles .

 2. Config = plain Python module, not YAML.

 3 real strategies today. YAML adds a loader + schema for zero clarity payoff. Extract to tools/campaign_config.py ; reporter + autopilot import it.

 3. Direct function call, not subprocess.

 Autopilot exposes run_autopilot() ; agent/main.py imports and calls it. Matches _run_daily_signal_scan shape (main.py:4042). No stdout parsing.

 4. Safety fuse = sum(active throttles).

 Not a magic 100. Fuse can't cap below config. If phase-a says LQD=10 others=0, fuse=10.

 5. Pool refresh: scheduled Monday + self-heal at age >8 days.

 Monday rebuild is routine. If Monday's rebuild fails and Tue/Wed/Thu also fail, Friday's run sees age >8d and rebuilds itself. Never silently runs on stale data.

 6. Per-prospect freshness + cohort-exclusivity checks at add-time (correctness, not just polish).

 Before POST: (a) find_prospect_by_email → if last Activity At < 90d , skip recent_activity ; (b) check the prospect's current sequence States → if already in any sequence tagged dre-2026-spring , skip

 already_in_dre_cohort . Without (b), a lead could be in LQD and TC-MS simultaneously — the cadences would collide and tank reply rate. This is the single highest-leverage correctness check in the whole system.

 7. Tag disqualified rows, don't drop silently.

 Pool metadata carries counts per reason ( no_state , no_tz , recent_activity , already_in_dre_cohort , etc.). Telegram summary surfaces them. Silent drops are debugging hell.

 8. adds_last_24h uses simple 24h-back filter.

 Outreach's throttle Max Adds Per Day day boundary isn't documented. 24h-back is strictly conservative, tz-math-free, and self-correcting within a cycle.

 9. Preview-before-live gate.

 --preview CLI mode writes a CSV of the exact next batch (email, company, verified_school, bucket, target sequence name). Steven eyeballs 10 rows BEFORE typing /autopilot_live . Catches systematic classifier

 regressions at the only moment that matters.

 10. Live flag persisted, reset path explicit.

 data/campaign_autopilot_state.json stores {"live_mode": bool} . /autopilot_live sets true; /autopilot_dry sets false; kill-switch file disables regardless. Bot restart preserves live mode (deploys are routine;

 reverting on every deploy is annoying not safe). Safety lives in the kill-switch + /autopilot_dry .

 11. Per-day state file, auto-archive ≥30 days old.

 data/dre_autopilot_2026-04-18.state.json — fresh file per day. Yesterday's stuck plans stay as a read-only record; they don't get retried and they don't bleed into today's run. Cleanup is one line at start of

 each run.

 12. Telegram formatter takes names, not IDs.

 Structural Rule 19 enforcement: no formatter parameter is typed as an int that could be a sequence ID. If a maintainer adds one, type-check catches it before ship.

 13. Sleep cadence 60–120s between POSTs.

 10 adds × 120s = 20 min wall time. Finishes before the 08:00 leadership_scan tick.

 14. Telegram summary only lists strategies that did something.

 Pristine unconfigured strategies ( pool_source=None ) are silent. Noise is the enemy of an always-on notification.

 Architecture

 agent/main.py poll loop

 └─> scheduler.check() → "campaign_autopilot" at 07:00 CST weekday

 └─> _run_campaign_autopilot()

 └─> tools.campaign_autopilot.run_autopilot(dry_run=<persisted flag>)

 ├─> tools.campaign_config.STRATEGIES

 ├─> scripts.campaign_budget_status.build_strategy_report() (reuse)

 ├─> tools.campaign_pool.get_or_build_pool(strategy, refresh=<Mon|age>8d>)

 │ ├─> tools.lead_filters.classify_sf_leads_to_dre_buckets()

 │ │ └─> tools.grade_level_detector.* (pure)

 │ └─> tools.timezone_lookup.state_to_timezone

 ├─> per sequence with throttle>0:

 │ need = throttle - adds_last_24h

 │ candidates = pool[bucket][:need_×2_headroom]

 │ for each: find_prospect_by_email → freshness + cohort checks

 │ build LoadPlan list

 ├─> tools.prospect_loader.execute_load_plan(plans, dry_run, sleep=(60,120))

 └─> format_telegram_summary(report) → send_message()

 Files

 New (prerequisite — Rule 18 debt)

 tools/grade_level_detector.py — pure functions lifted from /tmp/sf_leads_dre_pass3.py + pass4.py :

 - detect_grade(company, verified) -> Literal["Elem","MS","HS","All-Grades","Unknown"]

 - map_grade_span(span) -> Optional[Literal["Elem","MS","HS","AllGrades"]]

 - is_virtual(company, verified) -> bool

 - is_homeschool(company) -> bool

 - homeschool_subsplit(company) -> str

 - is_code_ninjas(company) -> bool

 - normalize_name(name) -> str

 Zero I/O, pure functions, string inputs only.

 tools/lead_filters.py — fuzzy-match + classifier (lifted from /tmp/sf_leads_dre_pass5.py ):

 - TC_SOURCES: frozenset[str] (22 strings from memory/project_uni_lead_source_whitelist.md )

 - Territory Index Named Tuple (the 7 dicts from pass5)

 - build_territory_index_stateaware(svc) -> TerritoryIndex

 - fuzzy_match(company, state, idx) -> tuple[Optional[bucket], tag]

 - classify_row(row, idx) -> Optional[bucket_name] (None = excluded)

 - classify_sf_leads_to_dre_buckets(svc=None) -> Dre Pool Result

 - Dre Pool Result.buckets: dict[bucket, list[Lead Row]]

 - Dre Pool Result.excluded: dict[reason, int]

 Sheets I/O gated behind factory; tests mock it.

 New (autopilot proper)

 tools/campaign_config.py — plain Python STRATEGIES: dict[str, Strategy Config] . Typed Dict with: number , display , tier , weekly_budget , sequence_ids OR sequence_ids_loader , pool_source ( "sf_leads_dre" | None ),

 bucket_to_sequence_name (dict | None), throttle_profile_name .

 tools/campaign_pool.py —

 - PoolLead(dataclass) — strict superset of prospect_loader.Contact : same fields plus bucket: str , source_row_id: str for audit. email_confidence defaults to "VERIFIED" for SF-Leads source.

 - Strategy Pool — {buckets: dict[bucket, list[Pool Lead]], excluded: dict[reason, int], built_at, total_eligible} .

 - get_or_build_pool(strategy_key, *, force_refresh=False) -> StrategyPool :

 - If data/<key>_pool.jsonl exists AND age ≤ 8d AND not force → load.

 - Else build_pool() .

 - build_pool(strategy_key) -> StrategyPool :

 - Dispatches on pool_source . For "sf_leads_dre" : lead_filters.classify_sf_leads_to_dre_buckets() → per-row state → timezone_lookup.state_to_timezone → tag no_state / no_tz → JSONL.

 - NOTE: 90-day dormancy was applied at S72 upstream. At-add-time freshness check in autopilot handles drift since.

 - JSONL schema version in .meta.json sidecar ( schema_version: 1 ).

 tools/campaign_autopilot.py —

 - run_autopilot(strategy_key="all", *, dry_run, preview_only=False) -> AutopilotReport

 - Kill-switch check FIRST: Path("~/.claude/state/scout-campaign-autopilot-disabled").expanduser().exists() → returns disabled report.

 - Per-strategy flow:

 a. Pool load/refresh (Monday OR age>8d OR force).

 b. build_strategy_report(strategy) → per-sequence throttle , enabled .

 c. For each enabled sequence with throttle > 0 :

 - need = throttle - adds_last_24h(seq_id)

 - Pull need × 2 candidates from pool[bucket] (headroom for skips).

 - For each candidate:

 - find_prospect_by_email(email) → if exists, check last Activity At . <90d → skip recent_activity .

 - If prospect exists, check sequenceStates tags → if any tag in {"dre-2026-spring"} → skip already_in_dre_cohort .

 - Else build LoadPlan .

 - Stop at need plans.

 d. If preview_only : write CSV, return.

 e. Else execute_load_plan(plans, state_path=data/dre_autopilot_<today>.state.json, sleep_seconds=(60,120), dry_run=dry_run) .

 - format_telegram_summary(report, *, live: bool) -> str — accepts dicts keyed by sequence NAME (not id).

 - Archive state files >30d → data/archive/ .

 - CLI __main__ : --strategy , --dry-run / --live , --preview , --refresh-pool .

 agent/scheduler.py — __init__ : self._last_campaign_autopilot_date = None . In check() weekday block:

 if hour == 7 and minute == 0 :

 if self._last_campaign_autopilot_date != today:

 self._last_campaign_autopilot_date = today

 return "campaign_autopilot"

 agent/main.py —

 - _run_campaign_autopilot() handler modeled on _run_daily_signal_scan (try/except + Telegram error on failure + send summary on success).

 - Dispatch in the scheduler loop (grep for how "signal_scan" is matched).

 - Startup: load data/campaign_autopilot_state.json → _autopilot_live_flag: bool .

 - Slash commands /autopilot_live + /autopilot_dry (Rule 14 direct-dispatch pattern) — update flag + rewrite state file + reply confirmation.

 - In-memory _autopilot_running: bool ; /autopilot_live refuses with warning if already running.

 agent/CLAUDE.md — add "campaign_autopilot" to Scheduler event enum; document new /autopilot_live + /autopilot_dry commands.

 scripts/campaign_budget_status.py — from tools.campaign_config import STRATEGIES . CLI behavior unchanged. Delete the inline dict.

 .gitignore — data/*_pool.jsonl , data/*_pool.meta.json , data/dre_autopilot_*.state.json , data/campaign_autopilot_state.json , data/archive/ , vault/logs/campaign_autopilot.jsonl .

 Build sequence (6 commits)

 Highest-leverage correctness gates sit at commits 2 (classifier parity) and 5 (cohort-exclusivity + preview). Anything after an unreversed upstream mistake gets redone — hence this ordering.

 Commit 1 — tools/grade_level_detector.py + tests.

 Lift pure functions from /tmp/sf_leads_dre_pass3.py + pass4.py . scripts/test_grade_level_detector.py covers 30+ fixture rows (Elem/MS/HS/All/Virtual/Homeschool/CodeNinjas/Unknown edge cases).

 Verify: .venv/bin/python -m pytest scripts/test_grade_level_detector.py -v .

 Commit 2 — tools/lead_filters.py + tests + live parity check.

 Lift fuzzy-match + classifier from /tmp/sf_leads_dre_pass5.py . Unit tests with mocked Sheets service.

 Verify (live, must pass before merge): run classifier against production SF Leads + Territory Schools. Per-bucket counts must match pass5's measured baselines (pass5.py:44 PASS4 dict) within ±50 rows. Total

 must match 26,137 measured within ±300. Record exact deltas in commit message.

 Commit 3 — tools/campaign_config.py + reporter refactor.

 Extract STRATEGIES to new module; add autopilot keys ( pool_source , bucket_to_sequence_name , throttle_profile_name ) to DRE entry. Reporter imports from it.

 Verify: diff <(git stash; python scripts/campaign_budget_status.py --format json; git stash pop) <(python scripts/campaign_budget_status.py --format json) byte-identical.

 Commit 4 — tools/campaign_pool.py + scripts/build_campaign_pool.py .

 Pool builder writes JSONL + .meta.json . Rule-17 tagging (not dropping).

 Verify:

 - python scripts/build_campaign_pool.py --strategy dre writes data/dre_2026_spring_pool.jsonl .

 - wc -l data/dre_2026_spring_pool.jsonl within ±300 of 26,137 (after no_state + no_tz drops).

 - jq -c '. | {state, tz}' data/dre_2026_spring_pool.jsonl | grep -v '"state":"[A-Z][A-Z]"' | head → empty (every row has 2-char state).

 - .meta.json shows excluded-reason breakdown summing correctly.

 Commit 5 — tools/campaign_autopilot.py (core + preview + all correctness checks).

 Implements kill-switch check, need computation, per-candidate freshness + cohort-exclusivity checks, LoadPlan build, preview CSV, dry-run summary.

 Verify (multi-step):

 1. python -m tools.campaign_autopilot --dry-run --strategy dre → stdout shows Telegram-formatted summary. Grep grep -oE '\b20[1-4][0-9]\b' → empty (no raw IDs).

 2. python -m tools.campaign_autopilot --preview --strategy dre → writes /tmp/campaign_preview_<ts>.csv . Cat it. Manually verify:

 - 10 rows for LQD-Universal.

 - Each row has plausible bucket→sequence mapping.

 - No row has a .com email domain in company field (common classifier bug).

 - No row's email matches an address Steven recognizes as already-active.

 3. python -c "from tools.campaign_autopilot import run_autopilot; r = run_autopilot(dry_run=True); print(r.total_attempted, r.skipped_already_in_cohort, r.skipped_recent_activity)" → confirms the correctness

 checks actually fire on real data (non-zero skipped_* counts are expected and healthy).

 Commit 6 — scheduler wire + slash commands + persistent flag.

 agent/scheduler.py tick. agent/main.py handler + /autopilot_live + /autopilot_dry . Startup loads persisted flag. Error path: any run_autopilot exception → Telegram message with traceback summary.

 Verify (end-to-end ship):

 1. Restart Scout. data/campaign_autopilot_state.json does not exist → flag defaults to False → dry-run mode.

 2. Trigger handler directly: python -c "import asyncio; from agent.main import _run_campaign_autopilot; asyncio.run(_run_campaign_autopilot())" → Telegram gets [DRY RUN] summary.

 3. Inspect vault/logs/campaign_autopilot.jsonl last line: mode=dry_run , strategies.dre.sequences.LQD-Universal.attempted=10, succeeded=0 .

 4. Run --preview CLI locally. Eyeball 10 rows. Spot-check 2 emails in Outreach UI — confirm they're real leads not already active.

 5. Send /autopilot_live in Telegram → confirmation reply → state file updated → cat data/campaign_autopilot_state.json shows {"live_mode": true} .

 6. Re-trigger handler → Telegram gets [LIVE] summary → audit log shows succeeded=10 .

 7. Outreach UI: LQD-Universal has 10 new active prospects. Each prospect record has populated State + Timezone.

 8. Send /autopilot_dry → back to dry-run.

 9. Wait for next 07:00 CST weekday tick → autopilot fires on its own in dry-run mode. Steven sends /autopilot_live when ready for autonomous live runs.

 Telegram format

 🤖 Campaign Autopilot — 2026-04-18 07:00 CST

 [DRY RUN — /autopilot_live to enable real adds]

 DRE (Dormant Re-Engage) · Tier 1

 Sends 7d: 47/266 (17.7%), 219 headroom

 Profile: phase-a (held)

 Would add 10 → LQD-Universal (cohort: 397 left)

 Pool: 26,137 eligible (built Mon 07:02 CST)

 Tier 1 TOTAL: 47/1,063, 1,016 headroom

 Live mode: [LIVE] , Would add → Added . No other strategies printed (pristine unconfigured strategies silent).

 Audit log

 vault/logs/campaign_autopilot.jsonl — one line per run:

 { "ts_utc" : "..." , "ts_cst" : "..." , "mode" : "dry_run|live|disabled|error" ,

 "strategies" : { "dre" : { "budget_7d" : 266 , "sends_7d" : 47 , "headroom" : 219 ,

 "sequences" : { "LQD-Universal" : { "throttle" : 10 , "adds_last_24h" : 0 ,

 "need" : 10 , "attempted" : 10 , "succeeded" : 10 ,

 "skipped_rule17" : 0 , "skipped_recent_activity" : 3 ,

 "skipped_already_in_dre_cohort" : 1 , "skipped_dedup" : 0 , "failed" : 0 }},

 "pool_built_at" : "..." , "pool_excluded" : { "no_state" : 312 , "no_tz" : 4 }}},

 "errors" : []}

 Risks

 ┌───────────────────────────────────────────────────────┬────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐

 │ Risk │ Mitigation │

 ├───────────────────────────────────────────────────────┼────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤

 │ Classifier regression routes leads to wrong cohort │ Commit 2 live parity (±50/bucket, ±300 total). Commit 5 --preview CSV eyeballed before first live run │

 ├───────────────────────────────────────────────────────┼────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤

 │ Lead already in another DRE sequence → cadence │ already_in_dre_cohort check via sequenceStates tag filter before each add │

 │ collision │ │

 ├───────────────────────────────────────────────────────┼────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤

 │ Lead had manual touch since S72 pool built → stepping │ find_prospect_by_email + last Activity At < 90d check before each add │

 │ on Steven │ │

 ├───────────────────────────────────────────────────────┼────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤

 │ Monday pool rebuild fails → Tue-Fri on stale data │ Self-heal: age >8d triggers rebuild on any run; surfaced in summary │

 ├───────────────────────────────────────────────────────┼────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤

 │ Bot crash mid-run orphans today's state file │ Per-day state file ( dre_autopilot_<today>.state.json ). Yesterday's stuck plans don't bleed into today; they're a read-only forensic record. Lost │

 │ │ adds: ≤10/day, recoverable manually │

 ├───────────────────────────────────────────────────────┼────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤

 │ throttle Max Adds Per Day day boundary ≠ our 24h-back │ Conservative: under-sends slightly on days after manual bursts. Revisit if audit log shows attempted > succeeded due to Outreach 429s │

 │ filter │ │

 ├───────────────────────────────────────────────────────┼────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤

 │ Rule 19 slip in Telegram output │ Formatter typed to take names, never IDs. Commit 5 grep check │

 ├───────────────────────────────────────────────────────┼────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤

 │ Railway deploy reverts live flag │ Persisted in data/campaign_autopilot_state.json ; survives restarts │

 ├───────────────────────────────────────────────────────┼────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤

 │ Concurrent command ( /drip_* ) during autopilot run → │ _autopilot_running flag logs warning; outreach_client already handles 429 retries │

 │ Outreach 429 │ │

 ├───────────────────────────────────────────────────────┼────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤

 │ Autopilot wall-time > 08:00 leadership_scan window │ Worst case 10 adds × 120s = 20 min. Hard cap: bail after 45 min │

 - prospect_loader.Contact.diocese_or_group field name is domain-specific. Autopilot passes the cohort bucket name ("TC-MS" etc.) through it — ugly but non-breaking. Rename to cohort_tag post-S74 with single

 caller update in diocesan_drip.py .

 - Morning brief doesn't yet include yesterday's autopilot outcome. Add after 2 weeks of live data exists to report against.

 - /apply_profile slash command for Steven to switch phase-a → proportional in one command. Defer until LQD actually drains.

 Non-goals (deferred)

 - tools/campaign_rebalancer.py . Defer until a real transition trigger exists.

 - YAML config. Defer until ~15 strategies.

 - Reply-rate-driven throttle tuning. Needs 2–3 weeks of live data.

 - Wiring pools for strategies #9 / #11. Config entries pool_source=None ; autopilot silently skips.

 - Generalizing classifier beyond DRE. Each strategy gets its own pool_source value + dispatch case in campaign_pool.build_pool when its turn comes.
