 ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── campaign-autopilot-worker ──

 - If the classifier is subtly wrong, 53 real prospects get a wrong-cohort email tonight. The --preview CSV I pulled earlier for the first 10 LQD-Universal looked reasonable but was not eyeballed for 53 rows.

 campaign-autopilot-worker

 - Outreach throttle at 55/day is a safety ceiling, not a planned send rate — the 53/day is set by autopilot-side budget math.

 ⏺ All 6 S74 commits pushed to origin/main.

 1. tools/grade_level_detector.py — 60 tests pass. Pure grade/virtual/homeschool detection.

 2. tools/lead_filters.py — 71 tests pass. Full 13-cohort DRE router. Live parity: 27,468 matched vs S72's 26,137 baseline (pre-filter delta).

 CSV.

 6. agent/scheduler.py + agent/main.py — 07:00 CST weekday tick, persisted /autopilot_live flag, /autopilot_dry , /autopilot_status commands, error path with Telegram nag.

 Next steps for going live:

 1. Railway auto-deploys from main.

 2. Tomorrow 07:00 CST → Telegram [DRY RUN] summary ("Would add 10 → LQD-Universal").

 3. Locally: .venv/bin/python -m tools.campaign_autopilot --preview --preview-out /tmp/dre_preview.csv → eyeball 10 rows.

 4. When ready: /autopilot_live in Telegram.

 ❯ OK i dont understand all of this but i assume this means it all got done and is working, correct? Is it all live and working now? Whats next?

 ⏺ Short answer: code is 100% done and pushed. It is DEPLOYED but deliberately in a safe "dry-run" mode. It is NOT sending real emails yet.

 Here's the state in plain terms:

 What's live right now

 - All 6 commits pushed to main. Railway is redeploying as we speak.

 - Tomorrow morning (Monday 2026-04-20 07:00 CST) Scout's scheduler will wake up, classify the 26k-lead DRE pool, and send you a Telegram message that says "Would add 10 → LQD-Universal" . Nothing actually gets

 POSTed to Outreach.

 - Every weekday at 07:00 CST thereafter: same thing. Pretend-run + Telegram summary. No real adds.

 What flips it from pretend to real

 - You send /autopilot_live in Telegram. That's it.

 - The very next 07:00 tick will POST 10 real prospects into LQD-Universal. Contacts start receiving sequence emails that day.

 - You can revert with /autopilot_dry at any time, or check mode with /autopilot_status .

 1. Watch tomorrow's dry-run summary land in Telegram. Make sure the numbers look right and no error message fires.

 2. Eyeball the batch before going live. From the terminal: .venv/bin/python -m tools.campaign_autopilot --preview --preview-out /tmp/dre_preview.csv then cat /tmp/dre_preview.csv . 10 rows of {email,

 first_name, last_name, company, state, target sequence}. If any row looks wrong (wrong cohort, email you already have a relationship with, etc.), tell me.

 3. When you're satisfied, send /autopilot_live in Telegram. Next 07:00 tick starts real sends into LQD-Universal at 10/day.

 4. After ~8 weeks LQD's 521 warm cohort drains. At that point, we flip from phase-a to proportional to open the other 12 sequences. That's a separate future session — not automated.

 Hard safety levers (hopefully never needed):

 - /autopilot_dry in Telegram → back to pretend mode.

 - touch ~/.claude/state/scout-campaign-autopilot-disabled → kill switch, autopilot refuses to run at all until removed.

 Bottom line: nothing ships to real prospects until you explicitly greenlight it. The system is sitting safely in test mode waiting for your go-ahead.

 ❯ why are we doing a dry run? I need to start sending out emails asap. why are we just adding 10 at a time? i need to add all 266 asap

 ⏺ Fair pushback on both. Let me explain the numbers and what your options actually are, then you tell me which.

 Why dry-run? I designed it as a safety net — you're the boss and can skip it. One /autopilot_live in Telegram flips it.

 Why 10/day and not 266? Two different numbers. 266 is your weekly Tier 1 DRE slice, not daily. 10/day × 5 weekdays = 50 adds/week — you're right, that's underutilizing the budget ~5x.

 The throttle is currently on profile phase-a (LQD-Universal 10/day, 12 other sequences paused at 0). That was the S73 plan to front-load the warmest 407 leads first. You're saying: skip the ramp, go to

 steady-state now.

 Math on "all 266 ASAP":

 - If you mean "use the full 266/week budget starting now" → switch to profile proportional . That spreads ~53/day across all 13 sequences: TC-Universal-Residual 14/day, TC-MS 11/day, TC-HS 10/day, TC-Elem

 10/day, smaller cohorts 1-2/day each. Hits 266/week immediately, opens all 13 cohorts.

 - If you mean "add 266 in ONE day" → that burns your entire weekly Tier 1 allowance on day 1 and silences Scout for the next 6 days to stay under the Outreach 5k rolling-7-day user cap. Bad idea. Don't

 recommend.

 I'm going to assume you mean the first one.

 Concrete plan — say "go" and I'll execute:

 1. .venv/bin/python scripts/create_dre_sequences.py --configure-throttles proportional → PATCHes all 13 sequences to proportional throttles. Real change to Outreach.

 2. Flip autopilot to live (just /autopilot_live in Telegram, or I can set the flag directly).

 3. Manually trigger one autopilot run right now (don't wait until Monday 07:00) → POSTs ~53 real prospects across the 13 sequences in the next ~20 minutes. Step 1 emails start firing immediately.

 4. Monday 07:00 and every weekday after: another ~53 adds automatically.

 Rough ramp: ~265 prospects/week entering sequences × 1 touch/week each → budget fills up in ~4 weeks to steady-state of ~1,064 active prospects sending ~266 emails/week.

 Confirm "go" and I'll execute all 3 steps right now. You'll see the proportional throttle PATCH output first, then the batch of ~53 real adds with skip counts per sequence.

 ❯ ok you said "that burns your entire weekly Tier 1 allowance on day 1" . that is not correct. it owuld burn my 266 alotted to #12 of tier 1. I have 266 emails also alotted to each of the other 3 for teir 1

 which is #9,10, and 11, if i am not mistaken. I want to get all 266 of #12 going asap. You can split the 266 up between tues, weds and thurs for the sends so all 266 dont go out all on tuesday next week. Of

 that 266 being sent for #12 it should be front loaded with the LQD leads then as those drain out it will be backfilled by the automation you just built with all the 12 other sequence/lead substrategies.

 Does that clear things up

 ⏺ You're right, I was wrong about the budget math. #12 has its own 266/week slice. Apologies.

 Two genuine design questions before I execute — need your call:

 1. Days: Tue/Wed/Thu only, or spread Mon-Fri?

 The autopilot fires every weekday at 07:00. Three options:

 - A. Mon-Fri: LQD throttle = 53/day → 265/week, gentle spread.

 - B. Tue/Wed/Thu only: LQD throttle = 89/day, and I manually zero the throttle Mon + Fri each week. One extra chore per week.

 - C. Tue/Wed/Thu only via autopilot-side day gate: small code change (~30 min) so scheduler only fires the autopilot on those 3 days. Cleaner long-term.

 2. "Front-load LQD then auto-backfill from the other 12" — two ways to do this:

 - Manual switch : LQD set to high, others at 0. Every morning I check pool counts; when LQD bucket drops below ~100, I run --configure-throttles proportional . ~1 minute of work, ~2 weeks out.

 - Priority-fill autopilot : I add a priority_order to config and smarten the autopilot so it fills LQD first each day, and the moment LQD bucket empties it automatically redirects that same day's allowance to

 TC-Universal-Residual, then TC-MS, etc. Zero manual intervention ever. ~2-3 hours of work, ships this session.

 The priority-fill option is strictly better if you want this fully hands-off. The manual option is zero extra work today + one quick check in two weeks.

 Tell me:

 - Days (A/B/C)?

 - Backfill (manual/priority-fill)?

 Once you answer, I execute — throttle PATCH, go live, and if you picked "execute this week" I manually fire the first autopilot run after Monday or Tuesday 07:00 fires the scheduled one.

 ❯ 1. the weekly email allotment should be split accros the days of the schedule for each sequence. whatever days are on the schedule are the days that you split them by. So even if you added them now they

 wouldnt send until the schedule opens up if im not mistaken.

 2. priorrity fill for sure.

 YOu need to make a note and remember about the budget math. youve made that mistake a few times now

 ⏺ Noted. Saving the budget-math memory first so I stop making this mistake:

 Read 1 file, recalled 1 memory, wrote 3 memories (ctrl+o to expand)

 ⏺ Memory locked. Now on your actual answers:

 On #1 (splitting): Makes sense. LQD's schedule is Hot Lead Mon-Fri (5 schedule days), so 266/5 = ~53/day adds fits naturally with the daily scheduler tick. When priority-fill shifts to the TC sequences

 (Teacher schedule Tue-Thu), we still add ~53/day calendar-wise; Outreach holds them and naturally bunches the sends onto Tue/Wed/Thu. Total sent stays 266/week regardless.

 ⏺ Code is committed. Now the live-state changes. These modify Outreach and will send real emails — need your explicit "go" before I execute:

 Plan:

 1. PATCH throttles. Set throttle Max Adds Per Day = 55 on all 13 DRE sequences (currently LQD=10, 12 others=0). This is a real Outreach write on sequences 2030-2042.

 2. Manual autopilot run NOW. Fires priority-fill live → up to 53 prospects POSTed to LQD-Universal tonight. Wall time ~15-25 min (60-120s sleeps between POSTs). Step 1 emails queue per schedule 51 (Mon-Fri) →

 first sends Monday morning.

 3. Flip autopilot to live. Persisted /autopilot_live flag → True. Every Mon-Fri 07:00 CST: another priority-fill run. LQD drains over ~10 working days; then TC-Universal-Residual picks up automatically.

 - Today (Fri): 53 adds → LQD-Universal (then 468 LQD left)

 - Mon 07:00: 53 more → LQD (415 left)

 - Tue 07:00: 53 more (362 left)

 - Wed: 53 (309 left)

 - Thu: 53 (256 left)

 - Total first full week sent: ~265 emails all from LQD

 Correctness gates still fire on every candidate:

 - skip recent_activity if last touched <90d (preserves your manual outreach)

 - skip already_in_dre_cohort if prospect's in another DRE sequence

 Risks:

 - If the classifier is subtly wrong, 53 real prospects get a wrong-cohort email tonight. The --preview CSV I pulled earlier for the first 10 LQD-Universal looked reasonable but was not eyeballed for 53 rows.

 - Outreach throttle at 55/day is a safety ceiling, not a planned send rate — the 53/day is set by autopilot-side budget math.

 Say "go" and I execute all 3 steps in sequence and stream progress back to you. Or "preview 53" and I'll --preview the full batch first so you eyeball before live.

 Ready to code?

 Here is Claude's plan:
 ╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌
 S74 — First Live-Run Pre-Flight Verification + Execution

 Context

 The S74 autopilot (6 commits, pushed) is code-complete and tested in dry-run. Before the first REAL POST to Outreach ever fires, the classifier-regression risk needs a programmatic audit — not a CSV eyeball

 punted to Steven. For LQD-Universal specifically the risk is bounded (pure whitelist-based routing — no grade fuzzy-match), but a full verification also doubles as the pattern for every future first-live-run

 of every future strategy. Building it once means never eyeballing a 50-row CSV again.

 Goal today:

 1. Pre-flight every candidate in the first 53-lead batch through programmatic checks.

 2. If the batch is clean, execute the throttle PATCH + live autopilot run tonight.

 3. 53 LQD-Universal prospects land in Outreach; Step 1 emails ship Monday morning per schedule 51 (Hot Lead Mon-Fri).

 Design

 Phase A — Pre-flight verification (read-only)

 New one-shot: scripts/verify_autopilot_batch.py . Reusable across future strategies (not LQD-specific).

 Input flags: --strategy dre --cohort LQD-Universal --n 60 --verbose . Emits a structured text report to stdout; exit 0 on clean, 1 on systematic regression.

 For each of the first ~60 candidates (~15% headroom over the 53/day budget to absorb hard-skips):

 ROUTING CORRECTNESS (hard abort on failure — classifier bug):

 - Fetch the SF Leads row for this email. Confirm Lead Source (case-insensitive) ∈ tools.lead_filters.LQD_SOURCES . Any miss = classifier is writing wrong leads into wrong buckets → HALT.

 HARD-SKIP (autopilot would already skip — pre-count for my visibility):

 - tools.outreach_client.find_prospect_by_email(email) — per S72 framework 99.83% should exist. If not found, this lead never touched Outreach → can't "re-engage" → flag not_in_outreach .

 - _get_prospect_touched_at < 90d → touched_within_90d .

 - _prospect_has_active_dre_cohort_state → already_in_dre_cohort .

 SOFT-FLAG (my judgment call before ship):

 - Email shape: regex ^[\w.+-]+@[\w-]+\.[\w.-]+$ . Typo heuristic: TLDs .con , .cpm , .nte , .orge . Flag — do not hard-skip (we saw .con typos in earlier preview).

 - Name encoding: first/last contains ? or non-ASCII-extended chars → flag encoding_issue .

 - Company field is actually an email address ( @ in string) → flag company_is_email .

 - Active-account cross-ref via csv_importer.get_active_accounts() + csv_importer.fuzzy_match_name() . If lead's company fuzzy-matches an active account with Deal Level = "district" → BUG 5 risk → flag

 active_district_match (CSMs handle these).

 CLEAN: passed all three groups.

 Report shape:

 PRE-FLIGHT — DRE LQD-Universal first 60 candidates

 CLASSIFIER AUDIT: 60/60 Lead Source in LQD_SOURCES ✓

 OUTREACH LOOKUP: 60/60 prospects checked

 BUCKETS:

 CLEAN (would send): N

 HARD-SKIP: M

 ├─ touched_within_90d: x

 ├─ already_in_dre_cohort: y

 └─ not_in_outreach: z

 SOFT-FLAG: K

 ├─ email_typo_tld: a

 ├─ encoding_issue: b

 ├─ company_is_email: c

 └─ active_district_match: d

 CLEAN sample (first 5):

 1. first last <email> — CompanyX, TX (prospect 12345, touched 2024-08-01)

 ...

 SOFT-FLAG details:

 email=... flag=active_district_match matched="Plano ISD" (Deal Level=district)

 email=... flag=email_typo_tld ".con" → suspected typo, would probably bounce

 ...

 Decision rules (I apply, not Steven):

 - Classifier-audit failures > 0 → HALT. Investigate. Do not send.

 - CLEAN ≥ 45 AND SOFT-FLAG count ≤ 5 AND no systematic pattern → proceed to Phase B.

 - CLEAN < 45 OR systematic SOFT-FLAG pattern → pause, report findings to Steven, decide per-flag.

 Phase B — Live execution (only after Phase A passes)

 Four live writes, reported to Steven one at a time:

 1. Throttle PATCH — bump throttle Max Adds Per Day from 10/0 to 55 across all 13 DRE sequences (2030–2042). 55 = daily-budget 53 + 2 rounding headroom + Outreach-side safety ceiling. Reuses

 tools.outreach_client._api_patch . Either via an extension to scripts/create_dre_sequences.py::THROTTLE_PROFILES (new profile autopilot-55 ) or a one-shot inline PATCH loop.

 2. Persist live flag — write {"live_mode": true} to data/campaign_autopilot_state.json . Confirms in agent/main.py 's startup load path (already implemented).

 3. Manual live autopilot run — python -m tools.campaign_autopilot --strategy dre --live . Priority-fills LQD-Universal up to the 53-lead budget; autopilot's own correctness gates re-fire (belt + suspenders with

 Phase A). Wall time ~15–25 min. verify_sequence_active=True in execute_load_plan gates the actual POSTs.

 4. Post-run report to Steven — succeeded/skipped/failed counts per cohort, plus first-day cohort-remaining, plus "next autopilot tick fires Mon 07:00 CST auto."

 Files

 New (Phase A):

 - scripts/verify_autopilot_batch.py — ~180 lines. Reads data/dre_pool.jsonl , runs 3-layer checks, emits text report. No writes.

 - scripts/create_dre_sequences.py — add AUTOPILOT_ADDS_PER_DAY = {name: 55 for name in 13 cohorts} entry to THROTTLE_PROFILES . 10-line addition. Optional — a one-shot inline PATCH works too, but the profile

 entry is reusable.

 - data/campaign_autopilot_state.json — {"live_mode": true} .

 - data/dre_autopilot_2026-04-17.state.json — created by execute_load_plan .

 - data/dre_2026_spring.state.json — throttle values updated in the "current_throttles" section by configure_throttles .

 - tools.lead_filters.LQD_SOURCES — authoritative whitelist

 - tools.outreach_client.find_prospect_by_email — prospect lookup

 - tools.outreach_client._api_get for /prospects/<id> → touchedAt

 - tools.campaign_autopilot._get_prospect_touched_at / _is_recent_activity / _prospect_has_active_dre_cohort_state

 - tools.csv_importer.get_active_accounts + fuzzy_match_name — active-account cross-ref

 - tools.campaign_pool.load_pool — reads the existing JSONL

 - scripts/create_dre_sequences.py::configure_throttles — throttle PATCH loop

 Verification (of the verification)

 Before acting on the pre-flight report:

 1. python scripts/verify_autopilot_batch.py --strategy dre --cohort LQD-Universal --n 60 — run & read output.

 2. Sanity: CLASSIFIER AUDIT should be 60/60 for LQD (trivially, since LQD is whitelist-exact). If even one miss, stop.

 3. Sanity: OUTREACH LOOKUP should be 60/60 — if any API calls failed, retry before interpreting counts.

 4. If HARD-SKIP > 20 (>33% of batch), cohort has worse-than-expected decay — investigate whether the pool builder's excluded_homeschool_* / parked_* buckets are leaking dormant rows into LQD.

 5. If SOFT-FLAG active_district_match fires multiple times, BUG 5 may need a stronger up-front filter — pause and ask Steven.

 Risks

 ┌────────────────────────────────────────────────────┬───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐

 │ Risk │ Mitigation │

 ├────────────────────────────────────────────────────┼───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤

 │ Active-account fuzzy match false-positive pulls │ Fuzzy threshold 0.8 (strict). False-positive = manual review. False-negative = autopilot's correctness gates still catch. Acceptable │

 │ legitimate LQD leads │ │

 ├────────────────────────────────────────────────────┼───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤

 │ Outreach API rate limit during 53-lead × 3-call │ Low risk at ~2 rps, ~90s wall. Outreach client handles 429 internally │

 │ verification (~160 calls) │ │

 ├────────────────────────────────────────────────────┼───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤

 │ Phase B throttle PATCH races a concurrent manual │ Low probability (he's watching). PATCH is idempotent — rerunning same profile is a no-op │

 │ sequence change by Steven │ │

 ├────────────────────────────────────────────────────┼───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤

 │ Execute_load_plan wall-time > morning-brief window │ Tonight's manual run is 16:51+25min=17:16 CDT, well before tomorrow's 09:15. Fine. Monday scheduled run at 07:00 finishes by 07:25, well before brief │

 │ at 09:15 CST │ │

 ├────────────────────────────────────────────────────┼───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤

 │ PROSPECT not-in-Outreach for LQD — means SF │ not_in_outreach rows skipped by autopilot (find_prospect_by_email returns None, autopilot treats as "new create" path — but create_prospect will │

 │ captured the lead but Outreach never got it │ fire. This is the one path where autopilot does create-new. Phase A will flag the count so I can decide: skip them or let create_prospect fire │

 └────────────────────────────────────────────────────┴───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

 Explicit non-goals

 - Baking Phase A verification into every autopilot run (today is a first-live gate; Monday's scheduler-fired run uses the autopilot's existing in-line correctness gates only).

 - Changing the autopilot's priority-fill logic (shipped correctly; Phase B exercises it).

 - Extending SOFT-FLAG set to classifier-heuristic checks (those matter for TC grade sequences, not LQD; defer to those first-live-runs).

 Execution sequence (what I'll do after you approve)

 1. Write scripts/verify_autopilot_batch.py .

 2. Run it. Read the report. Report to Steven: N clean, M hard-skip, K soft-flag.

 3. PAUSE — present findings. If systematic, stop. If clean, ask for green light on Phase B.

 4. On green light: PATCH throttles, flip live flag, run autopilot live, stream progress.

 5. Post final count + first-sends-Monday note.

 ❯ T ell Claude what to change

 Refine this plan. Your job is to pressure-test it honestly — keep what's
 genuinely strong, rebuild what isn't, and hand back something I can execute
 with full confidence.
 Act as a ruthless, senior-level technical reviewer and architect. Be direct,

 specific, and opinionated. No hedging, no diplomacy, no soft language around

 real problems. If something is wrong, say it's wrong and say why. But the

 inverse also holds: if something is right, leave it alone. Do not rewrite solid

 work just to look thorough — churn for its own sake is its own kind of

 failure. The bar for changing something is that the new version is meaningfully

 better, not merely different.

 happen because the reviewer started reacting before they finished

 understanding.

 1. Challenge the foundation. Is the overall approach actually right, or is it

 just the first thing that came to mind? Interrogate the architecture, the

 framing, and the core assumptions before touching details. If there's a

 fundamentally better angle — different architecture, different sequencing,

 different tradeoffs, a smaller problem hiding inside the big one — say so and

 restructure around it. Do not anchor to your first answer because you already

 committed to it on the page.

 2. Stress-test every layer as if you're executing it tomorrow. Walk the plan

 end to end and ask: could I actually sit down and build this without hitting a

 wall? Find the hidden assumptions, the vague steps, the dependency tangles, the

 places where things will quietly break at the edges, the spots where "it'll

 just work" is doing real load-bearing work. For every problem: name it, explain

 why it happens, explain what it costs, and give the fix. Diagnosis without

 prescription is useless.

 3. Pressure-test every technical decision against the best available knowledge.

 Architecture, tooling, patterns, data modeling, sequencing — for each one, ask

 whether it's genuinely the strongest option or just the most familiar. Weigh

 it against what actually holds up in production, not what's popular in

 tutorials or trending this quarter. Where real tradeoffs exist between

 competing approaches, surface them honestly and take a clear position on which

 one I should pick and why. No "it depends" cop-outs.

 4. Optimize for the highest-quality result, reached as fast and efficiently as

 possible. Every step should be pulling its weight toward a correct, durable,

 high-quality outcome — not just toward motion. Ruthlessly cut anything that

 adds time without adding quality, and ruthlessly add anything that protects

 quality even if it costs a little time upfront. Sequence the work so that the

 decisions and pieces with the highest leverage on the final result come first,

 so that mistakes get caught as early and cheaply as possible, and so that

 nothing downstream has to be redone because something upstream was rushed.

 Where two paths exist, pick the one that gets to the correct answer with the

 least wasted motion — and say why. Speed comes from not doing the wrong work,

 not from skipping the right work.

 5. Cut the fat, keep the muscle. Depth is welcome. Redundancy, vagueness, and

 ceremony are not. Every element earns its place or it goes. If a step would

 make me stop and ask "what does this actually mean in practice," either make it

 concrete or delete it. If two steps do the same work, collapse them. If a

 section exists because it sounds thorough rather than because it changes the

 outcome, kill it.

 6. Fill the gaps a senior engineer would catch in a design review. What's

 missing that looks minor now and will hurt later? Edge cases, error handling,

 failure modes, prerequisites, migration paths, ordering constraints, and the

 easy-to-punt decisions that get expensive to reverse. Surface them now, while

 they're cheap.

 7. Rewrite the full plan from scratch. Do not patch the original — rebuild it

 with everything above baked in. Make it comprehensive, precise, and immediately

 actionable. Preserve the parts of the original that were already strong —

 rebuilding does not mean discarding what worked. The goal is the best possible

 plan, not the most rewritten one. Then, at the end, list the most important

 changes you made and explain the reasoning behind each one so I can judge

 whether your thinking holds up.

 Take your time. Think deeply. This is the version I'm going to execute from —

 make it the best one you're capable of producing.

 ⏺ Short answer: code is 100% done and pushed. It is DEPLOYED but deliberately in a safe "dry-run" mode. It is NOT sending real emails yet.

 Here's the state in plain terms:

 What's live right now

 - All 6 commits pushed to main. Railway is redeploying as we speak.

 - Tomorrow morning (Monday 2026-04-20 07:00 CST) Scout's scheduler will wake up, classify the 26k-lead DRE pool, and send you a Telegram message that says "Would add 10 → LQD-Universal" . Nothing actually gets

 POSTed to Outreach.

 - Every weekday at 07:00 CST thereafter: same thing. Pretend-run + Telegram summary. No real adds.

 What flips it from pretend to real

 - You send /autopilot_live in Telegram. That's it.

 - The very next 07:00 tick will POST 10 real prospects into LQD-Universal. Contacts start receiving sequence emails that day.

 - You can revert with /autopilot_dry at any time, or check mode with /autopilot_status .

 1. Watch tomorrow's dry-run summary land in Telegram. Make sure the numbers look right and no error message fires.

 2. Eyeball the batch before going live. From the terminal: .venv/bin/python -m tools.campaign_autopilot --preview --preview-out /tmp/dre_preview.csv then cat /tmp/dre_preview.csv . 10 rows of {email,

 first_name, last_name, company, state, target sequence}. If any row looks wrong (wrong cohort, email you already have a relationship with, etc.), tell me.

 3. When you're satisfied, send /autopilot_live in Telegram. Next 07:00 tick starts real sends into LQD-Universal at 10/day.

 4. After ~8 weeks LQD's 521 warm cohort drains. At that point, we flip from phase-a to proportional to open the other 12 sequences. That's a separate future session — not automated.

 Hard safety levers (hopefully never needed):

 - /autopilot_dry in Telegram → back to pretend mode.

 - touch ~/.claude/state/scout-campaign-autopilot-disabled → kill switch, autopilot refuses to run at all until removed.

 Bottom line: nothing ships to real prospects until you explicitly greenlight it. The system is sitting safely in test mode waiting for your go-ahead.

 ❯ why are we doing a dry run? I need to start sending out emails asap. why are we just adding 10 at a time? i need to add all 266 asap

 ⏺ Fair pushback on both. Let me explain the numbers and what your options actually are, then you tell me which.

 Why dry-run? I designed it as a safety net — you're the boss and can skip it. One /autopilot_live in Telegram flips it.

 Why 10/day and not 266? Two different numbers. 266 is your weekly Tier 1 DRE slice, not daily. 10/day × 5 weekdays = 50 adds/week — you're right, that's underutilizing the budget ~5x.

 The throttle is currently on profile phase-a (LQD-Universal 10/day, 12 other sequences paused at 0). That was the S73 plan to front-load the warmest 407 leads first. You're saying: skip the ramp, go to

 steady-state now.

 Math on "all 266 ASAP":

 - If you mean "use the full 266/week budget starting now" → switch to profile proportional . That spreads ~53/day across all 13 sequences: TC-Universal-Residual 14/day, TC-MS 11/day, TC-HS 10/day, TC-Elem

 10/day, smaller cohorts 1-2/day each. Hits 266/week immediately, opens all 13 cohorts.

 - If you mean "add 266 in ONE day" → that burns your entire weekly Tier 1 allowance on day 1 and silences Scout for the next 6 days to stay under the Outreach 5k rolling-7-day user cap. Bad idea. Don't

 recommend.

 I'm going to assume you mean the first one.

 Concrete plan — say "go" and I'll execute:

 1. .venv/bin/python scripts/create_dre_sequences.py --configure-throttles proportional → PATCHes all 13 sequences to proportional throttles. Real change to Outreach.

 2. Flip autopilot to live (just /autopilot_live in Telegram, or I can set the flag directly).

 3. Manually trigger one autopilot run right now (don't wait until Monday 07:00) → POSTs ~53 real prospects across the 13 sequences in the next ~20 minutes. Step 1 emails start firing immediately.

 4. Monday 07:00 and every weekday after: another ~53 adds automatically.

 Rough ramp: ~265 prospects/week entering sequences × 1 touch/week each → budget fills up in ~4 weeks to steady-state of ~1,064 active prospects sending ~266 emails/week.

 Confirm "go" and I'll execute all 3 steps right now. You'll see the proportional throttle PATCH output first, then the batch of ~53 real adds with skip counts per sequence.

 which is #9,10, and 11, if i am not mistaken. I want to get all 266 of #12 going asap. You can split the 266 up between tues, weds and thurs for the sends so all 266 dont go out all on tuesday next week. Of

 that 266 being sent for #12 it should be front loaded with the LQD leads then as those drain out it will be backfilled by the automation you just built with all the 12 other sequence/lead substrategies.

 Does that clear things up

 ⏺ You're right, I was wrong about the budget math. #12 has its own 266/week slice. Apologies.

 Two genuine design questions before I execute — need your call:

 1. Days: Tue/Wed/Thu only, or spread Mon-Fri?

 The autopilot fires every weekday at 07:00. Three options:

 - A. Mon-Fri: LQD throttle = 53/day → 265/week, gentle spread.

 - B. Tue/Wed/Thu only: LQD throttle = 89/day, and I manually zero the throttle Mon + Fri each week. One extra chore per week.

 - C. Tue/Wed/Thu only via autopilot-side day gate: small code change (~30 min) so scheduler only fires the autopilot on those 3 days. Cleaner long-term.

 2. "Front-load LQD then auto-backfill from the other 12" — two ways to do this:

 - Manual switch : LQD set to high, others at 0. Every morning I check pool counts; when LQD bucket drops below ~100, I run --configure-throttles proportional . ~1 minute of work, ~2 weeks out.

 - Priority-fill autopilot : I add a priority_order to config and smarten the autopilot so it fills LQD first each day, and the moment LQD bucket empties it automatically redirects that same day's allowance to

 TC-Universal-Residual, then TC-MS, etc. Zero manual intervention ever. ~2-3 hours of work, ships this session.

 The priority-fill option is strictly better if you want this fully hands-off. The manual option is zero extra work today + one quick check in two weeks.

 Tell me:

 - Days (A/B/C)?

 - Backfill (manual/priority-fill)?

 Once you answer, I execute — throttle PATCH, go live, and if you picked "execute this week" I manually fire the first autopilot run after Monday or Tuesday 07:00 fires the scheduled one.

 ⏺ Memory locked. Now on your actual answers:

 On #1 (splitting): Makes sense. LQD's schedule is Hot Lead Mon-Fri (5 schedule days), so 266/5 = ~53/day adds fits naturally with the daily scheduler tick. When priority-fill shifts to the TC sequences

 (Teacher schedule Tue-Thu), we still add ~53/day calendar-wise; Outreach holds them and naturally bunches the sends onto Tue/Wed/Thu. Total sent stays 266/week regardless.

 52 ( or equivalent). Informational only; autopilot

 53 never auto-switches profiles

 54 + priority_order : cohort-name list . Autopilot priority-fills

 55 + the strategy 's daily budget in this order,

 56 + draining the top sequence before moving on

 ⎿   [main e1e42fc] feat(autopilot): priority-fill across cohorts within a strategy

 2 files changed, 71 insertions(+), 28 deletions(-)

 ⏺ Code is committed. Now the live-state changes. These modify Outreach and will send real emails — need your explicit "go" before I execute:

 Plan:

 1. PATCH throttles. Set throttle Max Adds Per Day = 55 on all 13 DRE sequences (currently LQD=10, 12 others=0). This is a real Outreach write on sequences 2030-2042.

 2. Manual autopilot run NOW. Fires priority-fill live → up to 53 prospects POSTed to LQD-Universal tonight. Wall time ~15-25 min (60-120s sleeps between POSTs). Step 1 emails queue per schedule 51 (Mon-Fri) →

 first sends Monday morning.

 3. Flip autopilot to live. Persisted /autopilot_live flag → True. Every Mon-Fri 07:00 CST: another priority-fill run. LQD drains over ~10 working days; then TC-Universal-Residual picks up automatically.

 - Today (Fri): 53 adds → LQD-Universal (then 468 LQD left)

 - Mon 07:00: 53 more → LQD (415 left)

 - Tue 07:00: 53 more (362 left)

 - Wed: 53 (309 left)

 - Thu: 53 (256 left)

 - Total first full week sent: ~265 emails all from LQD

 Correctness gates still fire on every candidate:

 - skip recent_activity if last touched <90d (preserves your manual outreach)

 - skip already_in_dre_cohort if prospect's in another DRE sequence

 Risks:

 - If the classifier is subtly wrong, 53 real prospects get a wrong-cohort email tonight. The --preview CSV I pulled earlier for the first 10 LQD-Universal looked reasonable but was not eyeballed for 53 rows.

 - Outreach throttle at 55/day is a safety ceiling, not a planned send rate — the 53/day is set by autopilot-side budget math.

 Say "go" and I execute all 3 steps in sequence and stream progress back to you. Or "preview 53" and I'll --preview the full batch first so you eyeball before live.

 ❯ Enter plan mode and really think carefully about how to do this. That first risk you listed can easily be mitigated by you doing the work to look at them yourself.

 ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── campaign-autopilot-worker ──
 ⏸ plan mode on (shift+tab to cycle)

 ✻ thought for 97s)

 ⏺ Thinking harder about the whole thing before rewriting.

 Biggest foundation reconsideration I missed on the first pass: Schedule 51 (Hot Lead Mon-Fri) doesn't send on weekends. If we add 53 leads to LQD-Universal at 5pm Friday, Step 1 emails queue and first-fire
 actual business benefit over letting Monday's scheduler do it. Cut that complexity.

 ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── campaign-autopilot-worker ──

 ⏸ plan mode on (shift+tab to cycle)

 Second miss: I said not_in_outreach candidates should pass through to create_prospect . Wrong. DRE is by definition "dormant re-engage" — if they're not in Outreach, they were never engaged, so "re-engage" is
 meaningless. These should HARD-SKIP, not create new prospects in a re-engagement campaign.

 ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── campaign-autopilot-worker ──

 ⏸ plan mode on (shift+tab to cycle)

 Third miss: I didn't wire filter_leads_against_active_accounts into the pool builder (commit 4). S72 framework assumed it was there (that's why baseline was 26,137 post-filter vs our 27,467 pre-filter). This
 is a real gap — my Phase A SOFT-FLAG catches it for the first batch but the systemic fix belongs in pool_build. Flag as documented debt + make Phase A catch it per-batch until fixed.

 ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── campaign-autopilot-worker ──

 ⏸ plan mode on (shift+tab to cycle)

 Fourth miss: n=60 is too tight. At observed ~20% hard-skip rate, need ~80 probed to get 53 clean.

 campaign-autopilot-worker

 ⏸ plan mode on (shift+tab to cycle)

 Fifth miss: Plan needs to be a complete cold-start handoff since Steven said "save and don't start until next session." No conversation-state dependency. Writing the script itself happens NEXT session.

 Ready to code?

 Here is Claude's plan:
 ╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌
 S75 — First Live-Run Pre-Flight + Go-Live (DRE / LQD-Universal)

 Intended session: S75. This plan file is a cold-start handoff — it assumes no conversation state from S74.

 Where S74 left us

 - 6 S74 commits pushed to origin/main : grade_level_detector, lead_filters, campaign_config, campaign_pool, campaign_autopilot, scheduler wiring. Autopilot code is live in the bot but _autopilot_live_flag

 defaults False, so the scheduler's 07:00 CST Mon-Fri tick fires it in dry-run only.

 - Priority-fill logic added (commit e1e42fc ). DRE strategy daily budget = 266/5 = 53 adds/day, filled LQD-Universal → TC-Universal-Residual → TC-MS → ... per STRATEGIES["dre"].priority_order .

 - data/dre_pool.jsonl exists with 27,467 measured eligible DRE leads (521 in LQD-Universal cohort). Built 2026-04-17T21:18 UTC.

 - Outreach throttle still on phase-a : LQD=10/day, 12 others=0. Autopilot's 53/day budget is currently capped at 10 by Outreach throttle.

 - Budget math locked: each Tier 1 strategy (#9/#10/#11/#12) has its OWN 266/week allocation. See memory/feedback_tier1_budget_is_per_strategy.md .

 Goal of this session

 Ship the first REAL POSTs to Outreach from the S74 autopilot, safely:

 1. Programmatically verify the exact 53-lead batch that autopilot would send next.

 2. If clean, flip throttles + live flag. Let Monday 07:00's scheduled autopilot tick do the actual adds.

 3. Steven wakes Monday to a [LIVE] Telegram summary; 53 Step 1 emails ship during Monday's schedule-51 window.

 Foundation decisions

 1. Schedule alignment eliminates the manual-trigger path.

 LQD-Universal uses schedule 51 (Hot Lead Mon-Fri, no weekend sends). Adds made at 5pm Friday and adds made at 07:00 Monday both result in Step 1 firing Monday. So:

 - ❌ "PATCH + flip live + manually trigger autopilot CLI tonight + forward output to Telegram" — engineering cost for zero send-timing benefit.

 - ✅ "PATCH + flip live + walk away — Monday 07:00 auto-fires the live run" — same outcome, no manual plumbing.

 If this session happens AFTER Monday's 07:00 tick (e.g. Monday afternoon), fall back to one-line manual trigger — see Phase C.

 2. Phase A verification is a one-shot gate, not a permanent autopilot layer.

 The SOFT-FLAG checks (active-account cross-ref, data-quality heuristics) are first-live-run guards. After this session, they're subsumed by two things: (a) autopilot's existing in-line correctness gates handle

 the HARD layer on every run, (b) the filter_leads_against_active_accounts pool-level fix (S76 debt, see Risks) fills the SOFT gap structurally. Don't bloat autopilot with SOFT layer — build the one-shot, run

 it once, move on.

 3. not_in_outreach is a HARD-SKIP, not a pass-through.

 DRE = dormant re-engage. If a lead doesn't exist in Outreach, they were never engaged → "re-engage" is a category error. Current autopilot code would fall through to create_prospect (creates new prospect +

 adds). Phase A treats not_in_outreach as HARD-SKIP and does NOT let autopilot create-new for this cohort. Fix: a one-line guard in _process_strategy that skips candidates where find_prospect_by_email returns

 None.

 4. Sample size n=80 not 60.

 Observed HARD-SKIP rate in S74 dry-run of first 10: 2/10 = 20%. For a 53-lead budget we need ~67 that pass HARD gates. 80 gives ~15% headroom.

 5. Plan file is the complete handoff.

 No "remember from last session" assumptions. Everything S75 needs is either in this plan, in memory/*.md , or in committed code referenced by path.

 Phase A — Pre-flight verification (READ-ONLY)

 Build

 New file: scripts/verify_autopilot_batch.py (~200 lines).

 Flags: --strategy dre --cohort LQD-Universal --n 80 [--verbose] . Returns exit 0 on clean, 1 on systematic regression. No writes anywhere.

 Per-candidate checks (run against first N pool leads in insertion order — same order autopilot would pick)

 Layer 1 — ROUTING CORRECTNESS (hard abort on even one failure):

 - Pull the live SF Leads row by email. Assert Lead Source (lowercased, trimmed) ∈ tools.lead_filters.LQD_SOURCES . Any miss = classifier wrote wrong bucket → HALT (do not proceed to Phase B).

 - Implementation: reuse tools.lead_filters.read_sf_leads_rows to fetch the full sheet once, build an email→row dict, iterate.

 Layer 2 — HARD-SKIP (would be skipped by autopilot's existing gates + our new not_in_outreach fix):

 - tools.outreach_client.find_prospect_by_email(email) → if None → not_in_outreach (HARD-SKIP; see Foundation #3).

 - If exists: fetch /prospects/<id> raw; read attributes.touchedAt . If parsed < 90 days ago → touched_within_90d .

 - _api_get("/sequenceStates", filter[prospect][id]=X, include=sequence) — scan included sequences' attributes.tags for "dre-2026-spring" . Present → already_in_dre_cohort .

 Layer 3 — SOFT-FLAG (my judgment; does NOT auto-skip, flag for review):

 - Email validity regex: ^[A-Za-z0-9._%+-]+@[A-Za-z0-9.-]+\.[A-Za-z]{2,}$ . Fail → email_malformed .

 - Typo TLD heuristic: endswith in {".con", ".cpm", ".nte", ".orge", ".ocm"} → email_typo_tld .

 - Encoding: first_name OR last_name contains ? OR any codepoint > 0x017F → encoding_issue (catches the ?????? row seen in dry-run).

 - Company-is-email: @ appears in company string → company_is_email .

 - Active-account cross-ref: load tools.csv_importer.get_active_accounts() once. For each lead, csv_importer.fuzzy_match_name(normalize_name(lead.company), [normalize_name(a["Name"]) for a in accounts],

 threshold=0.8) . If match AND matched-account Deal Level == "district" → active_district_match (BUG 5 territory; CSMs handle districts). If match but Deal Level != district → active_account_match (logged, not a

 skip signal — school-level customer isn't a CSM issue).

 Output format

 Single text report to stdout:

 PRE-FLIGHT — DRE / LQD-Universal / first 80 candidates

 LAYER 1 ROUTING: 80/80 in LQD_SOURCES ✓ (else: HALT)

 LAYER 2 OUTREACH: 80/80 lookups completed ✓

 COUNTS:

 CLEAN (ready): N

 HARD-SKIP: M

 ├─ not_in_outreach: a

 ├─ touched_within_90d: b

 └─ already_in_dre_cohort: c

 SOFT-FLAG (review needed): K

 ├─ email_malformed: d

 ├─ email_typo_tld: e

 ├─ encoding_issue: f

 ├─ company_is_email: g

 └─ active_district_match: h

 First 5 CLEAN:

 email · Full Name · CompanyX · TX · prospect 12345 · touched YYYY-MM-DD

 ...

 All SOFT-FLAG rows (details):

 email · flag=active_district_match · matched_account="Plano ISD" (district)

 ...

 Decision rules (I apply — no Steven input needed unless systematic)

 ┌────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┬─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐

 │ Observation │ Action │

 ├────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┼─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤

 │ Layer 1 routing failure (any) │ HALT. Investigate classifier. │

 ├────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┼─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤

 │ CLEAN ≥ 53 AND SOFT-FLAG ≤ 5 AND no single SOFT reason ≥ 3 │ Proceed to Phase B │

 ├────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┼─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤

 │ CLEAN < 53 │ Increase n; rerun. If still < 53 at n=120, investigate cohort decay │

 ├────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┼─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤

 │ active_district_match count ≥ 3 │ Pause; list them for Steven's eyeball — BUG 5 surface │

 ├────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┼─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤

 │ Any SOFT reason showing up in >10% of sample │ Systematic — investigate before proceeding │

 └────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┴─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

 Phase B — Live execution (after Phase A passes)

 Step 0 — Preconditions (all must be true)

 - Kill switch absent: ~/.claude/state/scout-campaign-autopilot-disabled does not exist.

 - Repo clean on main ; no uncommitted changes to tools/campaign_*.py or scripts/ .

 - Pool age < 8d: jq -r '.built_at' data/dre_pool.meta.json within a week.

 - Phase A exited 0 AND Steven greenlit the findings.

 Step 1 — PATCH throttles (the only pre-live write)

 Extend scripts/create_dre_sequences.py::THROTTLE_PROFILES with a new named profile:

 AUTOPILOT_ADDS_PER_DAY = {k: 55 for k in PROPORTIONAL_ADDS_PER_DAY}

 THROTTLE_PROFILES[ "autopilot-55" ] = AUTOPILOT_ADDS_PER_DAY

 55 = budget 53 + 2 rounding headroom. Outreach's throttle serves as belt + suspenders ceiling; autopilot's 53/day budget is the real governor.

 Run: .venv/bin/python scripts/create_dre_sequences.py --configure-throttles autopilot-55 . PATCHes all 13 DRE sequences (2030-2042). Reuses the existing hardened PATCH loop.

 Verify by reading back: .venv/bin/python scripts/campaign_budget_status.py --strategy dre --format json | jq '.[0].sequences[].throttle_per_day' — all 13 should show 55.

 Step 2 — Apply the not_in_outreach guard to autopilot

 Small edit in tools/campaign_autopilot.py::_process_strategy candidate loop:

 prospect = find_prospect_by_email(lead.email)

 if prospect is None :

 seq_result.skipped_no_pool_candidates += 1 # or add a new counter

 continue

 # existing freshness + cohort checks follow

 Add a new counter skipped_not_in_outreach to Sequence Autopilot Result so the audit log distinguishes it.

 Commit: fix(autopilot): skip not_in_outreach for DRE cohort (S75 live-run gate) .

 Step 3 — Flip live flag

 Two equivalent paths:

 - A. /autopilot_live in Telegram (preferred — exercises the real production code path).

 - B. echo '{"live_mode": true}' > data/campaign_autopilot_state.json (fallback if Telegram is unavailable; requires bot restart to pick up).

 Verify: cat data/campaign_autopilot_state.json → {"live_mode": true} .

 Step 4 — Let the scheduler fire it OR manually trigger

 Case A: session ends before Monday 07:00 CDT.

 - Do nothing further. Monday's scheduled tick fires the live run autonomously.

 - Steven receives [LIVE] Telegram summary Monday ~07:20 CDT.

 Case B: session is Monday after 07:00 CDT (scheduled tick already passed).

 - Manually trigger via the bot's own handler (not CLI — we want Telegram-path parity):

 - Send /autopilot_status in Telegram → confirms LIVE mode.

 - Then either wait for Tuesday OR manually invoke. The cleanest one-liner is a small addition in S75: a /autopilot_run_now slash command that calls asyncio.create_task(_run_campaign_autopilot()) . Ship it

 with the Step 2 commit.

 - Telegram summary arrives ~20-25 min later.

 Step 5 — Post-run validation

 After the live run completes (Monday 07:20 CDT or Case B trigger):

 1. Grep vault/logs/campaign_autopilot.jsonl | tail -1 | jq — confirm mode=live , strategies.dre.sequences["DRE 2026 Spring — LQD-Universal"].succeeded within expected range.

 2. Outreach UI spot-check: LQD-Universal sequence shows ~53 new active sequenceStates, all with mailbox_id=11 and tag dre-2026-spring .

 3. Expected counts:

 - Succeeded: ~50-53 (cohort has 521, plenty of headroom).

 - skipped_recent_activity: ~5-12 (the correctness-gate dynamic baseline).

 - skipped_already_in_dre_cohort: 0 for first run (no one else is in DRE yet).

 - skipped_not_in_outreach: small — Phase A caught the systemic case.

 - failed: 0. Any >0 → investigate Outreach errors in audit log.

 Files touched this session

 New:

 - scripts/verify_autopilot_batch.py — Phase A pre-flight.

 - scripts/create_dre_sequences.py — add autopilot-55 throttle profile.

 - tools/campaign_autopilot.py — not_in_outreach HARD-SKIP guard + counter.

 - (Optional, only if Case B applies) agent/main.py — /autopilot_run_now slash command.

 Written-but-not-committed state files (gitignored):

 - data/campaign_autopilot_state.json — {"live_mode": true} .

 - data/dre_autopilot_2026-04-2X.state.json — created by Monday's run.

 - tools.lead_filters.LQD_SOURCES — authoritative whitelist

 Risks

 Explicit non-goals for S75

 - Baking Phase A verification into every autopilot run (first-live gate only).

 - Wiring filter_leads_against_active_accounts into pool_build (S76).

 - Adding Phase A checks for TC-* cohorts (classifier risk is higher there; separate first-live run per cohort when we open those).

 - Manual CLI autopilot run + Telegram forwarding (cut — schedule-51 timing makes it pointless).

 - Reply-rate-driven throttle tuning or profile auto-switching (future sessions with live data).

 ❯ OK i dont understand all of this but i assume this means it all got done and is working, correct? Is it all live and working now? Whats next?

 ⏺ Short answer: code is 100% done and pushed. It is DEPLOYED but deliberately in a safe "dry-run" mode. It is NOT sending real emails yet.

 Here's the state in plain terms:

 What's live right now

 - All 6 commits pushed to main. Railway is redeploying as we speak.

 - Tomorrow morning (Monday 2026-04-20 07:00 CST) Scout's scheduler will wake up, classify the 26k-lead DRE pool, and send you a Telegram message that says "Would add 10 → LQD-Universal" . Nothing actually gets

 POSTed to Outreach.

 - Every weekday at 07:00 CST thereafter: same thing. Pretend-run + Telegram summary. No real adds.

 What flips it from pretend to real

 - You send /autopilot_live in Telegram. That's it.

 - The very next 07:00 tick will POST 10 real prospects into LQD-Universal. Contacts start receiving sequence emails that day.

 - You can revert with /autopilot_dry at any time, or check mode with /autopilot_status .

 1. Watch tomorrow's dry-run summary land in Telegram. Make sure the numbers look right and no error message fires.

 2. Eyeball the batch before going live. From the terminal: .venv/bin/python -m tools.campaign_autopilot --preview --preview-out /tmp/dre_preview.csv then cat /tmp/dre_preview.csv . 10 rows of {email,

 first_name, last_name, company, state, target sequence}. If any row looks wrong (wrong cohort, email you already have a relationship with, etc.), tell me.

 3. When you're satisfied, send /autopilot_live in Telegram. Next 07:00 tick starts real sends into LQD-Universal at 10/day.

 4. After ~8 weeks LQD's 521 warm cohort drains. At that point, we flip from phase-a to proportional to open the other 12 sequences. That's a separate future session — not automated.

 Hard safety levers (hopefully never needed):

 - /autopilot_dry in Telegram → back to pretend mode.

 - touch ~/.claude/state/scout-campaign-autopilot-disabled → kill switch, autopilot refuses to run at all until removed.

 Bottom line: nothing ships to real prospects until you explicitly greenlight it. The system is sitting safely in test mode waiting for your go-ahead.

 ❯ why are we doing a dry run? I need to start sending out emails asap. why are we just adding 10 at a time? i need to add all 266 asap

 ⏺ Fair pushback on both. Let me explain the numbers and what your options actually are, then you tell me which.

 Why dry-run? I designed it as a safety net — you're the boss and can skip it. One /autopilot_live in Telegram flips it.

 Why 10/day and not 266? Two different numbers. 266 is your weekly Tier 1 DRE slice, not daily. 10/day × 5 weekdays = 50 adds/week — you're right, that's underutilizing the budget ~5x.

 The throttle is currently on profile phase-a (LQD-Universal 10/day, 12 other sequences paused at 0). That was the S73 plan to front-load the warmest 407 leads first. You're saying: skip the ramp, go to

 steady-state now.

 Math on "all 266 ASAP":

 - If you mean "use the full 266/week budget starting now" → switch to profile proportional . That spreads ~53/day across all 13 sequences: TC-Universal-Residual 14/day, TC-MS 11/day, TC-HS 10/day, TC-Elem

 10/day, smaller cohorts 1-2/day each. Hits 266/week immediately, opens all 13 cohorts.

 - If you mean "add 266 in ONE day" → that burns your entire weekly Tier 1 allowance on day 1 and silences Scout for the next 6 days to stay under the Outreach 5k rolling-7-day user cap. Bad idea. Don't

 recommend.

 I'm going to assume you mean the first one.

 Concrete plan — say "go" and I'll execute:

 1. .venv/bin/python scripts/create_dre_sequences.py --configure-throttles proportional → PATCHes all 13 sequences to proportional throttles. Real change to Outreach.

 2. Flip autopilot to live (just /autopilot_live in Telegram, or I can set the flag directly).

 3. Manually trigger one autopilot run right now (don't wait until Monday 07:00) → POSTs ~53 real prospects across the 13 sequences in the next ~20 minutes. Step 1 emails start firing immediately.

 4. Monday 07:00 and every weekday after: another ~53 adds automatically.

 Rough ramp: ~265 prospects/week entering sequences × 1 touch/week each → budget fills up in ~4 weeks to steady-state of ~1,064 active prospects sending ~266 emails/week.

 Confirm "go" and I'll execute all 3 steps right now. You'll see the proportional throttle PATCH output first, then the batch of ~53 real adds with skip counts per sequence.

 which is #9,10, and 11, if i am not mistaken. I want to get all 266 of #12 going asap. You can split the 266 up between tues, weds and thurs for the sends so all 266 dont go out all on tuesday next week. Of

 that 266 being sent for #12 it should be front loaded with the LQD leads then as those drain out it will be backfilled by the automation you just built with all the 12 other sequence/lead substrategies.

 Does that clear things up

 ⏺ You're right, I was wrong about the budget math. #12 has its own 266/week slice. Apologies.

 Two genuine design questions before I execute — need your call:

 1. Days: Tue/Wed/Thu only, or spread Mon-Fri?

 The autopilot fires every weekday at 07:00. Three options:

 - A. Mon-Fri: LQD throttle = 53/day → 265/week, gentle spread.

 - B. Tue/Wed/Thu only: LQD throttle = 89/day, and I manually zero the throttle Mon + Fri each week. One extra chore per week.

 - C. Tue/Wed/Thu only via autopilot-side day gate: small code change (~30 min) so scheduler only fires the autopilot on those 3 days. Cleaner long-term.

 2. "Front-load LQD then auto-backfill from the other 12" — two ways to do this:

 - Manual switch : LQD set to high, others at 0. Every morning I check pool counts; when LQD bucket drops below ~100, I run --configure-throttles proportional . ~1 minute of work, ~2 weeks out.

 - Priority-fill autopilot : I add a priority_order to config and smarten the autopilot so it fills LQD first each day, and the moment LQD bucket empties it automatically redirects that same day's allowance to

 TC-Universal-Residual, then TC-MS, etc. Zero manual intervention ever. ~2-3 hours of work, ships this session.

 The priority-fill option is strictly better if you want this fully hands-off. The manual option is zero extra work today + one quick check in two weeks.

 Tell me:

 - Days (A/B/C)?

 - Backfill (manual/priority-fill)?

 Once you answer, I execute — throttle PATCH, go live, and if you picked "execute this week" I manually fire the first autopilot run after Monday or Tuesday 07:00 fires the scheduled one.

 first sends Monday morning.

 ⏺ Thinking harder about the whole thing before rewriting.

 Biggest foundation reconsideration I missed on the first pass: Schedule 51 (Hot Lead Mon-Fri) doesn't send on weekends. If we add 53 leads to LQD-Universal at 5pm Friday, Step 1 emails queue and first-fire

 actual business benefit over letting Monday's scheduler do it. Cut that complexity.

 Second miss: I said not_in_outreach candidates should pass through to create_prospect . Wrong. DRE is by definition "dormant re-engage" — if they're not in Outreach, they were never engaged, so "re-engage" is

 meaningless. These should HARD-SKIP, not create new prospects in a re-engagement campaign.

 Third miss: I didn't wire filter_leads_against_active_accounts into the pool builder (commit 4). S72 framework assumed it was there (that's why baseline was 26,137 post-filter vs our 27,467 pre-filter). This

 is a real gap — my Phase A SOFT-FLAG catches it for the first batch but the systemic fix belongs in pool_build. Flag as documented debt + make Phase A catch it per-batch until fixed.

 Fourth miss: n=60 is too tight. At observed ~20% hard-skip rate, need ~80 probed to get 53 clean.

 Fifth miss: Plan needs to be a complete cold-start handoff since Steven said "save and don't start until next session." No conversation-state dependency. Writing the script itself happens NEXT session.

 ❯ OK i dont understand all of this but i assume this means it all got done and is working, correct? Is it all live and working now? Whats next?

 ⏺ Short answer: code is 100% done and pushed. It is DEPLOYED but deliberately in a safe "dry-run" mode. It is NOT sending real emails yet.

 Here's the state in plain terms:

 What's live right now

 - All 6 commits pushed to main. Railway is redeploying as we speak.

 - Tomorrow morning (Monday 2026-04-20 07:00 CST) Scout's scheduler will wake up, classify the 26k-lead DRE pool, and send you a Telegram message that says "Would add 10 → LQD-Universal" . Nothing actually gets

 POSTed to Outreach.

 - Every weekday at 07:00 CST thereafter: same thing. Pretend-run + Telegram summary. No real adds.

 What flips it from pretend to real

 - You send /autopilot_live in Telegram. That's it.

 - The very next 07:00 tick will POST 10 real prospects into LQD-Universal. Contacts start receiving sequence emails that day.

 - You can revert with /autopilot_dry at any time, or check mode with /autopilot_status .

 1. Watch tomorrow's dry-run summary land in Telegram. Make sure the numbers look right and no error message fires.

 2. Eyeball the batch before going live. From the terminal: .venv/bin/python -m tools.campaign_autopilot --preview --preview-out /tmp/dre_preview.csv then cat /tmp/dre_preview.csv . 10 rows of {email,

 first_name, last_name, company, state, target sequence}. If any row looks wrong (wrong cohort, email you already have a relationship with, etc.), tell me.

 3. When you're satisfied, send /autopilot_live in Telegram. Next 07:00 tick starts real sends into LQD-Universal at 10/day.

 4. After ~8 weeks LQD's 521 warm cohort drains. At that point, we flip from phase-a to proportional to open the other 12 sequences. That's a separate future session — not automated.

 Hard safety levers (hopefully never needed):

 - /autopilot_dry in Telegram → back to pretend mode.

 - touch ~/.claude/state/scout-campaign-autopilot-disabled → kill switch, autopilot refuses to run at all until removed.

 Bottom line: nothing ships to real prospects until you explicitly greenlight it. The system is sitting safely in test mode waiting for your go-ahead.

 ❯ why are we doing a dry run? I need to start sending out emails asap. why are we just adding 10 at a time? i need to add all 266 asap

 ⏺ Fair pushback on both. Let me explain the numbers and what your options actually are, then you tell me which.

 Why dry-run? I designed it as a safety net — you're the boss and can skip it. One /autopilot_live in Telegram flips it.

 Why 10/day and not 266? Two different numbers. 266 is your weekly Tier 1 DRE slice, not daily. 10/day × 5 weekdays = 50 adds/week — you're right, that's underutilizing the budget ~5x.

 The throttle is currently on profile phase-a (LQD-Universal 10/day, 12 other sequences paused at 0). That was the S73 plan to front-load the warmest 407 leads first. You're saying: skip the ramp, go to

 steady-state now.

 Math on "all 266 ASAP":

 - If you mean "use the full 266/week budget starting now" → switch to profile proportional . That spreads ~53/day across all 13 sequences: TC-Universal-Residual 14/day, TC-MS 11/day, TC-HS 10/day, TC-Elem

 10/day, smaller cohorts 1-2/day each. Hits 266/week immediately, opens all 13 cohorts.

 - If you mean "add 266 in ONE day" → that burns your entire weekly Tier 1 allowance on day 1 and silences Scout for the next 6 days to stay under the Outreach 5k rolling-7-day user cap. Bad idea. Don't

 recommend.

 I'm going to assume you mean the first one.

 Concrete plan — say "go" and I'll execute:

 1. .venv/bin/python scripts/create_dre_sequences.py --configure-throttles proportional → PATCHes all 13 sequences to proportional throttles. Real change to Outreach.

 2. Flip autopilot to live (just /autopilot_live in Telegram, or I can set the flag directly).

 3. Manually trigger one autopilot run right now (don't wait until Monday 07:00) → POSTs ~53 real prospects across the 13 sequences in the next ~20 minutes. Step 1 emails start firing immediately.

 4. Monday 07:00 and every weekday after: another ~53 adds automatically.

 Rough ramp: ~265 prospects/week entering sequences × 1 touch/week each → budget fills up in ~4 weeks to steady-state of ~1,064 active prospects sending ~266 emails/week.

 Confirm "go" and I'll execute all 3 steps right now. You'll see the proportional throttle PATCH output first, then the batch of ~53 real adds with skip counts per sequence.

 ⏺ You're right, I was wrong about the budget math. #12 has its own 266/week slice. Apologies.

 Two genuine design questions before I execute — need your call:

 1. Days: Tue/Wed/Thu only, or spread Mon-Fri?

 The autopilot fires every weekday at 07:00. Three options:

 - A. Mon-Fri: LQD throttle = 53/day → 265/week, gentle spread.

 - B. Tue/Wed/Thu only: LQD throttle = 89/day, and I manually zero the throttle Mon + Fri each week. One extra chore per week.

 - C. Tue/Wed/Thu only via autopilot-side day gate: small code change (~30 min) so scheduler only fires the autopilot on those 3 days. Cleaner long-term.

 2. "Front-load LQD then auto-backfill from the other 12" — two ways to do this:

 - Manual switch : LQD set to high, others at 0. Every morning I check pool counts; when LQD bucket drops below ~100, I run --configure-throttles proportional . ~1 minute of work, ~2 weeks out.

 - Priority-fill autopilot : I add a priority_order to config and smarten the autopilot so it fills LQD first each day, and the moment LQD bucket empties it automatically redirects that same day's allowance to

 TC-Universal-Residual, then TC-MS, etc. Zero manual intervention ever. ~2-3 hours of work, ships this session.

 The priority-fill option is strictly better if you want this fully hands-off. The manual option is zero extra work today + one quick check in two weeks.

 Tell me:

 - Days (A/B/C)?

 - Backfill (manual/priority-fill)?

 Once you answer, I execute — throttle PATCH, go live, and if you picked "execute this week" I manually fire the first autopilot run after Monday or Tuesday 07:00 fires the scheduled one.

 Plan:

 1. PATCH throttles. Set throttle Max Adds Per Day = 55 on all 13 DRE sequences (currently LQD=10, 12 others=0). This is a real Outreach write on sequences 2030-2042.

 first sends Monday morning.

 3. Flip autopilot to live. Persisted /autopilot_live flag → True. Every Mon-Fri 07:00 CST: another priority-fill run. LQD drains over ~10 working days; then TC-Universal-Residual picks up automatically.

 ⏺ Thinking harder about the whole thing before rewriting.

 Biggest foundation reconsideration I missed on the first pass: Schedule 51 (Hot Lead Mon-Fri) doesn't send on weekends. If we add 53 leads to LQD-Universal at 5pm Friday, Step 1 emails queue and first-fire

 actual business benefit over letting Monday's scheduler do it. Cut that complexity.

 Second miss: I said not_in_outreach candidates should pass through to create_prospect . Wrong. DRE is by definition "dormant re-engage" — if they're not in Outreach, they were never engaged, so "re-engage" is

 meaningless. These should HARD-SKIP, not create new prospects in a re-engagement campaign.

 Third miss: I didn't wire filter_leads_against_active_accounts into the pool builder (commit 4). S72 framework assumed it was there (that's why baseline was 26,137 post-filter vs our 27,467 pre-filter). This

 is a real gap — my Phase A SOFT-FLAG catches it for the first batch but the systemic fix belongs in pool_build. Flag as documented debt + make Phase A catch it per-batch until fixed.

 Fourth miss: n=60 is too tight. At observed ~20% hard-skip rate, need ~80 probed to get 53 clean.

 Fifth miss: Plan needs to be a complete cold-start handoff since Steven said "save and don't start until next session." No conversation-state dependency. Writing the script itself happens NEXT session.

 ⏺ Short answer: code is 100% done and pushed. It is DEPLOYED but deliberately in a safe "dry-run" mode. It is NOT sending real emails yet.

 Here's the state in plain terms:

 What's live right now

 - All 6 commits pushed to main. Railway is redeploying as we speak.

 - Tomorrow morning (Monday 2026-04-20 07:00 CST) Scout's scheduler will wake up, classify the 26k-lead DRE pool, and send you a Telegram message that says "Would add 10 → LQD-Universal" . Nothing actually gets

 POSTed to Outreach.

 - Every weekday at 07:00 CST thereafter: same thing. Pretend-run + Telegram summary. No real adds.

 What flips it from pretend to real

 - You send /autopilot_live in Telegram. That's it.

 - The very next 07:00 tick will POST 10 real prospects into LQD-Universal. Contacts start receiving sequence emails that day.

 - You can revert with /autopilot_dry at any time, or check mode with /autopilot_status .

 1. Watch tomorrow's dry-run summary land in Telegram. Make sure the numbers look right and no error message fires.

 2. Eyeball the batch before going live. From the terminal: .venv/bin/python -m tools.campaign_autopilot --preview --preview-out /tmp/dre_preview.csv then cat /tmp/dre_preview.csv . 10 rows of {email,

 first_name, last_name, company, state, target sequence}. If any row looks wrong (wrong cohort, email you already have a relationship with, etc.), tell me.

 3. When you're satisfied, send /autopilot_live in Telegram. Next 07:00 tick starts real sends into LQD-Universal at 10/day.

 4. After ~8 weeks LQD's 521 warm cohort drains. At that point, we flip from phase-a to proportional to open the other 12 sequences. That's a separate future session — not automated.

 Hard safety levers (hopefully never needed):

 - /autopilot_dry in Telegram → back to pretend mode.

 - touch ~/.claude/state/scout-campaign-autopilot-disabled → kill switch, autopilot refuses to run at all until removed.

 Bottom line: nothing ships to real prospects until you explicitly greenlight it. The system is sitting safely in test mode waiting for your go-ahead.

 ⏺ Fair pushback on both. Let me explain the numbers and what your options actually are, then you tell me which.

 Why dry-run? I designed it as a safety net — you're the boss and can skip it. One /autopilot_live in Telegram flips it.

 Why 10/day and not 266? Two different numbers. 266 is your weekly Tier 1 DRE slice, not daily. 10/day × 5 weekdays = 50 adds/week — you're right, that's underutilizing the budget ~5x.

 The throttle is currently on profile phase-a (LQD-Universal 10/day, 12 other sequences paused at 0). That was the S73 plan to front-load the warmest 407 leads first. You're saying: skip the ramp, go to

 steady-state now.

 Math on "all 266 ASAP":

 - If you mean "use the full 266/week budget starting now" → switch to profile proportional . That spreads ~53/day across all 13 sequences: TC-Universal-Residual 14/day, TC-MS 11/day, TC-HS 10/day, TC-Elem

 10/day, smaller cohorts 1-2/day each. Hits 266/week immediately, opens all 13 cohorts.

 - If you mean "add 266 in ONE day" → that burns your entire weekly Tier 1 allowance on day 1 and silences Scout for the next 6 days to stay under the Outreach 5k rolling-7-day user cap. Bad idea. Don't

 recommend.

 I'm going to assume you mean the first one.

 Concrete plan — say "go" and I'll execute:

 1. .venv/bin/python scripts/create_dre_sequences.py --configure-throttles proportional → PATCHes all 13 sequences to proportional throttles. Real change to Outreach.

 2. Flip autopilot to live (just /autopilot_live in Telegram, or I can set the flag directly).

 3. Manually trigger one autopilot run right now (don't wait until Monday 07:00) → POSTs ~53 real prospects across the 13 sequences in the next ~20 minutes. Step 1 emails start firing immediately.

 4. Monday 07:00 and every weekday after: another ~53 adds automatically.

 Rough ramp: ~265 prospects/week entering sequences × 1 touch/week each → budget fills up in ~4 weeks to steady-state of ~1,064 active prospects sending ~266 emails/week.

 Confirm "go" and I'll execute all 3 steps right now. You'll see the proportional throttle PATCH output first, then the batch of ~53 real adds with skip counts per sequence.

 ⏺ You're right, I was wrong about the budget math. #12 has its own 266/week slice. Apologies.

 Two genuine design questions before I execute — need your call:

 1. Days: Tue/Wed/Thu only, or spread Mon-Fri?

 The autopilot fires every weekday at 07:00. Three options:

 - A. Mon-Fri: LQD throttle = 53/day → 265/week, gentle spread.

 - B. Tue/Wed/Thu only: LQD throttle = 89/day, and I manually zero the throttle Mon + Fri each week. One extra chore per week.

 - C. Tue/Wed/Thu only via autopilot-side day gate: small code change (~30 min) so scheduler only fires the autopilot on those 3 days. Cleaner long-term.

 2. "Front-load LQD then auto-backfill from the other 12" — two ways to do this:

 - Manual switch : LQD set to high, others at 0. Every morning I check pool counts; when LQD bucket drops below ~100, I run --configure-throttles proportional . ~1 minute of work, ~2 weeks out.

 - Priority-fill autopilot : I add a priority_order to config and smarten the autopilot so it fills LQD first each day, and the moment LQD bucket empties it automatically redirects that same day's allowance to

 TC-Universal-Residual, then TC-MS, etc. Zero manual intervention ever. ~2-3 hours of work, ships this session.

 The priority-fill option is strictly better if you want this fully hands-off. The manual option is zero extra work today + one quick check in two weeks.

 Tell me:

 - Days (A/B/C)?

 - Backfill (manual/priority-fill)?

 Once you answer, I execute — throttle PATCH, go live, and if you picked "execute this week" I manually fire the first autopilot run after Monday or Tuesday 07:00 fires the scheduled one.

 Plan:

 1. PATCH throttles. Set throttle Max Adds Per Day = 55 on all 13 DRE sequences (currently LQD=10, 12 others=0). This is a real Outreach write on sequences 2030-2042.

 2. Manual autopilot run NOW. Fires priority-fill live → up to 53 prospects POSTed to LQD-Universal tonight. Wall time ~15-25 min (60-120s sleeps between POSTs). Step 1 emails queue per schedule 51 (Mon-Fri) →

 first sends Monday morning.

 3. Flip autopilot to live. Persisted /autopilot_live flag → True. Every Mon-Fri 07:00 CST: another priority-fill run. LQD drains over ~10 working days; then TC-Universal-Residual picks up automatically.

 - Today (Fri): 53 adds → LQD-Universal (then 468 LQD left)

 - Mon 07:00: 53 more → LQD (415 left)

 - Tue 07:00: 53 more (362 left)

 - Wed: 53 (309 left)

 - Thu: 53 (256 left)

 - Total first full week sent: ~265 emails all from LQD

 Correctness gates still fire on every candidate:

 - skip recent_activity if last touched <90d (preserves your manual outreach)

 - skip already_in_dre_cohort if prospect's in another DRE sequence

 Risks:

 - If the classifier is subtly wrong, 53 real prospects get a wrong-cohort email tonight. The --preview CSV I pulled earlier for the first 10 LQD-Universal looked reasonable but was not eyeballed for 53 rows.

 - Outreach throttle at 55/day is a safety ceiling, not a planned send rate — the 53/day is set by autopilot-side budget math.

 ⏺ Thinking harder about the whole thing before rewriting.

 Biggest foundation reconsideration I missed on the first pass: Schedule 51 (Hot Lead Mon-Fri) doesn't send on weekends. If we add 53 leads to LQD-Universal at 5pm Friday, Step 1 emails queue and first-fire

 actual business benefit over letting Monday's scheduler do it. Cut that complexity.

 Second miss: I said not_in_outreach candidates should pass through to create_prospect . Wrong. DRE is by definition "dormant re-engage" — if they're not in Outreach, they were never engaged, so "re-engage" is

 meaningless. These should HARD-SKIP, not create new prospects in a re-engagement campaign.

 Third miss: I didn't wire filter_leads_against_active_accounts into the pool builder (commit 4). S72 framework assumed it was there (that's why baseline was 26,137 post-filter vs our 27,467 pre-filter). This

 is a real gap — my Phase A SOFT-FLAG catches it for the first batch but the systemic fix belongs in pool_build. Flag as documented debt + make Phase A catch it per-batch until fixed.

 Fourth miss: n=60 is too tight. At observed ~20% hard-skip rate, need ~80 probed to get 53 clean.

 Fifth miss: Plan needs to be a complete cold-start handoff since Steven said "save and don't start until next session." No conversation-state dependency. Writing the script itself happens NEXT session.

 ⏺ You're right, I was wrong about the budget math. #12 has its own 266/week slice. Apologies.

 Two genuine design questions before I execute — need your call:

 1. Days: Tue/Wed/Thu only, or spread Mon-Fri?

 The autopilot fires every weekday at 07:00. Three options:

 - A. Mon-Fri: LQD throttle = 53/day → 265/week, gentle spread.

 - B. Tue/Wed/Thu only: LQD throttle = 89/day, and I manually zero the throttle Mon + Fri each week. One extra chore per week.

 - C. Tue/Wed/Thu only via autopilot-side day gate: small code change (~30 min) so scheduler only fires the autopilot on those 3 days. Cleaner long-term.

 2. "Front-load LQD then auto-backfill from the other 12" — two ways to do this:

 - Manual switch : LQD set to high, others at 0. Every morning I check pool counts; when LQD bucket drops below ~100, I run --configure-throttles proportional . ~1 minute of work, ~2 weeks out.

 - Priority-fill autopilot : I add a priority_order to config and smarten the autopilot so it fills LQD first each day, and the moment LQD bucket empties it automatically redirects that same day's allowance to

 TC-Universal-Residual, then TC-MS, etc. Zero manual intervention ever. ~2-3 hours of work, ships this session.

 The priority-fill option is strictly better if you want this fully hands-off. The manual option is zero extra work today + one quick check in two weeks.

 Tell me:

 - Days (A/B/C)?

 - Backfill (manual/priority-fill)?

 Once you answer, I execute — throttle PATCH, go live, and if you picked "execute this week" I manually fire the first autopilot run after Monday or Tuesday 07:00 fires the scheduled one.

 On #1 (splitting): Makes sense. LQD's schedule is Hot Lead Mon-Fri (5 schedule days), so 266/5 = ~53/day adds fits naturally with the daily scheduler tick. When priority-fill shifts to the TC sequences

 (Teacher schedule Tue-Thu), we still add ~53/day calendar-wise; Outreach holds them and naturally bunches the sends onto Tue/Wed/Thu. Total sent stays 266/week regardless.

 Plan:

 1. PATCH throttles. Set throttle Max Adds Per Day = 55 on all 13 DRE sequences (currently LQD=10, 12 others=0). This is a real Outreach write on sequences 2030-2042.

 2. Manual autopilot run NOW. Fires priority-fill live → up to 53 prospects POSTed to LQD-Universal tonight. Wall time ~15-25 min (60-120s sleeps between POSTs). Step 1 emails queue per schedule 51 (Mon-Fri) →

 first sends Monday morning.

 3. Flip autopilot to live. Persisted /autopilot_live flag → True. Every Mon-Fri 07:00 CST: another priority-fill run. LQD drains over ~10 working days; then TC-Universal-Residual picks up automatically.

 - Today (Fri): 53 adds → LQD-Universal (then 468 LQD left)

 - Mon 07:00: 53 more → LQD (415 left)

 - Tue 07:00: 53 more (362 left)

 - Wed: 53 (309 left)

 - Thu: 53 (256 left)

 - Total first full week sent: ~265 emails all from LQD

 Correctness gates still fire on every candidate:

 - skip recent_activity if last touched <90d (preserves your manual outreach)

 - skip already_in_dre_cohort if prospect's in another DRE sequence

 Risks:

 - If the classifier is subtly wrong, 53 real prospects get a wrong-cohort email tonight. The --preview CSV I pulled earlier for the first 10 LQD-Universal looked reasonable but was not eyeballed for 53 rows.

 - Outreach throttle at 55/day is a safety ceiling, not a planned send rate — the 53/day is set by autopilot-side budget math.

 ⏺ Thinking harder about the whole thing before rewriting.

 Biggest foundation reconsideration I missed on the first pass: Schedule 51 (Hot Lead Mon-Fri) doesn't send on weekends. If we add 53 leads to LQD-Universal at 5pm Friday, Step 1 emails queue and first-fire

 actual business benefit over letting Monday's scheduler do it. Cut that complexity.

 Second miss: I said not_in_outreach candidates should pass through to create_prospect . Wrong. DRE is by definition "dormant re-engage" — if they're not in Outreach, they were never engaged, so "re-engage" is

 meaningless. These should HARD-SKIP, not create new prospects in a re-engagement campaign.

 Third miss: I didn't wire filter_leads_against_active_accounts into the pool builder (commit 4). S72 framework assumed it was there (that's why baseline was 26,137 post-filter vs our 27,467 pre-filter). This

 is a real gap — my Phase A SOFT-FLAG catches it for the first batch but the systemic fix belongs in pool_build. Flag as documented debt + make Phase A catch it per-batch until fixed.

 Fourth miss: n=60 is too tight. At observed ~20% hard-skip rate, need ~80 probed to get 53 clean.

 Fifth miss: Plan needs to be a complete cold-start handoff since Steven said "save and don't start until next session." No conversation-state dependency. Writing the script itself happens NEXT session.

 - gitignored). Commit `c39c580`.
