 - target is the "got 15 min?" cliché). `allow_phrases=["15 minutes"]` passes through on both sides without relaxing the default for other callers.

 24 - - **Copy file NOT modified.** `docs/DRE_13_Sequences_Final_Copy_S72.md` stays as the frozen source-of-truth. Transformations are in-memory at build time.

 25 - - **Known POST-WRITE verifier warnings (both false-positive on this campaign, not creation failures):**

 26 - 1. "step 2 body contains banned dash characters ['–']" — the verifier fetches live template 43784 body and finds an en-dash in Steven's existing template content. Pre-existing; template 43784

 - was already deployed. Not Scout-actionable without editing 43784 directly.

 27 - 2. "step 5 body contains banned phrases ['15 minutes']" — `verify_sequence` doesn't yet accept `allow_phrases` (I only plumbed pre-write; post-write mirror is a small S74 patch). Sequences lan

 - ded correctly.

 28 - - **S74 first actions:** (1) Steven reviews and activates each of 13 sequences in Outreach UI (2017-2029). (2) Small library patch — thread `allow_phrases` through `verify_sequence` so future au

 - dits don't flag these as false positives. (3) Begin prospect-add via `find_prospect_by_email` + `add_prospect_to_sequence` (NOT `create_prospect` — 99.83% measured of DRE eligibles already exist

 - in Outreach). Rule 17 (state + tz) enforced by `validate_prospect_inputs`. Build cohort CSVs from the S72 filter artifacts preserved at `/tmp/sf_leads_dre_pass5.py` + friends.

 29 - - **Repo state:** S73 work committed across 3 commits (`3587807`, `c39c580`, + EOS wrap). Working tree clean except `.DS_Store`. To be pushed end-of-session.

 12 + - **6+1 S74 commits pushed to `origin/main`** (`49d9ad8` → `066e421` → `e1e42fc`): `tools/grade_level_detector.py` + `tools/lead_filters.py` (60+71 tests; promoted `/tmp/sf_leads_dre_pass3+4+5.p

 + y`), `tools/campaign_config.py` (`STRATEGIES` extracted from budget reporter; adds `pool_source` / `bucket_to_sequence_name` / `throttle_profile_name` / `priority_order`), `tools/campaign_pool.p

 + y` (`PoolLead` superset of `Contact`; JSONL + meta at `data/dre_pool.jsonl`; 15 tests), `tools/campaign_autopilot.py` (run_autopilot + priority-fill + preview CSV + kill-switch + Rule-19-clean f

 + ormatter), `agent/scheduler.py` 07:00 CST weekday tick, `agent/main.py` handler + `/autopilot_live` / `/autopilot_dry` / `/autopilot_status` slash commands + persisted flag at `data/campaign_aut

 + opilot_state.json`.

 13 + - **Live verification done:** dry-run autopilot on production produced 10 LQD-Universal LoadPlans w/ 2 correctly-skipped for `recent_activity`. Pool contains **27,467 measured eligible DRE leads

 + ** (521 LQD, 7273 TC-Universal-Residual, 5772 TC-MS, 5241 TC-HS, 5051 TC-Elem, INT 932, LIB 638, TC-Virtual 451, TC-District 450, INT-Teacher 432, TC-Teacher 390, IT-ReEngage 190, TC-All-Grades

 + 126). Delta vs S72 baseline 26,137 = +1,331 is the missing `filter_leads_against_active_accounts` pool-level filter (not wired in `campaign_pool.build_pool` — S76 debt, documented in S75 plan Ri

 + sks table).

 14 + - **Budget math correction baked into memory:** Each Tier 1 strategy (#9/#10/#11/#12) has its OWN 266/week allocation — NOT a shared pool. Claude miscommunicated this multiple times in S74. Memo

 + ry: `feedback_tier1_budget_is_per_strategy.md`. DRE daily budget = 266/5 = 53/day.

 15 + - **Priority-fill (commit `e1e42fc`):** autopilot walks `STRATEGIES["dre"].priority_order` (LQD → TC-U-Residual → TC-MS → TC-HS → TC-Elem → others) with a strategy-wide 53/day budget, draining t

 + he top cohort before spilling into the next. When LQD's 521 exhausts (~10 working days) adds flow automatically to TC-U-Residual etc.

 16 + - **Outreach throttle still `phase-a`:** LQD=10/day, 12 others=0. Autopilot's 53/day budget is capped at 10 until throttle is PATCHed to `autopilot-55` in S75.

 17 + - **Autopilot is LIVE in the bot but in dry-run mode.** `_autopilot_live_flag=False` default. Monday 07:00 CST scheduler will fire `_run_campaign_autopilot()` in dry-run → Telegram "[DRY RUN] Wo

 + uld add 10 → LQD-Universal" summary. Steven has NOT flipped live yet.

 18 + - **S75 plan approved and saved at `/Users/stevenadkins/.claude/plans/snuggly-wishing-cocke.md`.** Three-phase: (A) build+run `scripts/verify_autopilot_batch.py` pre-flight on first 80 LQD candi

 + cheduled tick auto-fires live run → ~53 adds to LQD-Universal, Step 1 sends ship Monday's schedule-51 window. No manual CLI trigger — schedule-51 Mon-Fri means "adds tonight" and "adds Monday 07

 ⏺ Thinking harder about the whole thing before rewriting.

 Biggest foundation reconsideration I missed on the first pass: Schedule 51 (Hot Lead Mon-Fri) doesn't send on weekends. If we add 53 leads to LQD-Universal at 5pm Friday, Step 1 emails queue and first-fire

 actual business benefit over letting Monday's scheduler do it. Cut that complexity.

 Second miss: I said not_in_outreach candidates should pass through to create_prospect . Wrong. DRE is by definition "dormant re-engage" — if they're not in Outreach, they were never engaged, so "re-engage" is

 meaningless. These should HARD-SKIP, not create new prospects in a re-engagement campaign.

 Third miss: I didn't wire filter_leads_against_active_accounts into the pool builder (commit 4). S72 framework assumed it was there (that's why baseline was 26,137 post-filter vs our 27,467 pre-filter). This

 is a real gap — my Phase A SOFT-FLAG catches it for the first batch but the systemic fix belongs in pool_build. Flag as documented debt + make Phase A catch it per-batch until fixed.

 Fourth miss: n=60 is too tight. At observed ~20% hard-skip rate, need ~80 probed to get 53 clean.

 Fifth miss: Plan needs to be a complete cold-start handoff since Steven said "save and don't start until next session." No conversation-state dependency. Writing the script itself happens NEXT session.

 ⏺ Code is committed. Now the live-state changes. These modify Outreach and will send real emails — need your explicit "go" before I execute:

 Plan:

 1. PATCH throttles. Set throttle Max Adds Per Day = 55 on all 13 DRE sequences (currently LQD=10, 12 others=0). This is a real Outreach write on sequences 2030-2042.

 2. Manual autopilot run NOW. Fires priority-fill live → up to 53 prospects POSTed to LQD-Universal tonight. Wall time ~15-25 min (60-120s sleeps between POSTs). Step 1 emails queue per schedule 51 (Mon-Fri) →

 first sends Monday morning.

 3. Flip autopilot to live. Persisted /autopilot_live flag → True. Every Mon-Fri 07:00 CST: another priority-fill run. LQD drains over ~10 working days; then TC-Universal-Residual picks up automatically.

 - Today (Fri): 53 adds → LQD-Universal (then 468 LQD left)

 - Mon 07:00: 53 more → LQD (415 left)

 - Tue 07:00: 53 more (362 left)

 - Wed: 53 (309 left)

 - Thu: 53 (256 left)

 - Total first full week sent: ~265 emails all from LQD

 Correctness gates still fire on every candidate:

 - skip recent_activity if last touched <90d (preserves your manual outreach)

 - skip already_in_dre_cohort if prospect's in another DRE sequence

 Risks:

 - If the classifier is subtly wrong, 53 real prospects get a wrong-cohort email tonight. The --preview CSV I pulled earlier for the first 10 LQD-Universal looked reasonable but was not eyeballed for 53 rows.

 - Outreach throttle at 55/day is a safety ceiling, not a planned send rate — the 53/day is set by autopilot-side budget math.

 Say "go" and I execute all 3 steps in sequence and stream progress back to you. Or "preview 53" and I'll --preview the full batch first so you eyeball before live.

 ❯ Enter plan mode and really think carefully about how to do this. That first risk you listed can easily be mitigated by you doing the work to look at them yourself.

 ⏺ Entering plan mode. You're right — I shouldn't punt classifier verification to you when I can do it systematically.

 ⏺ Thinking harder about the whole thing before rewriting.

 Biggest foundation reconsideration I missed on the first pass: Schedule 51 (Hot Lead Mon-Fri) doesn't send on weekends. If we add 53 leads to LQD-Universal at 5pm Friday, Step 1 emails queue and first-fire

 actual business benefit over letting Monday's scheduler do it. Cut that complexity.

 Second miss: I said not_in_outreach candidates should pass through to create_prospect . Wrong. DRE is by definition "dormant re-engage" — if they're not in Outreach, they were never engaged, so "re-engage" is

 meaningless. These should HARD-SKIP, not create new prospects in a re-engagement campaign.

 Third miss: I didn't wire filter_leads_against_active_accounts into the pool builder (commit 4). S72 framework assumed it was there (that's why baseline was 26,137 post-filter vs our 27,467 pre-filter). This

 is a real gap — my Phase A SOFT-FLAG catches it for the first batch but the systemic fix belongs in pool_build. Flag as documented debt + make Phase A catch it per-batch until fixed.

 Fourth miss: n=60 is too tight. At observed ~20% hard-skip rate, need ~80 probed to get 53 clean.

 Fifth miss: Plan needs to be a complete cold-start handoff since Steven said "save and don't start until next session." No conversation-state dependency. Writing the script itself happens NEXT session.
